observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:

a. Inventory Committee to continuously make utmost effort to conduct a
complete and detailed physical inventory of all the LGU’s properties and
prepare the RPCPPE in accordance with the guidelines and procedures
prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006;

b. CAO and the GSO to maintain complete and updated PPELCs and
complete PCs for all PPEs; and

c. GSO and the CAO to make a complete reconciliation of their respective
records and avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances,
including the disposition and derecognition of non-existing/missing PPE
and those without available records of accountability and adjust the books
accordingly, in order to come up with reliable PPE balances.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the OIC-City Accountant to reclassify erroneous entries on the
recording of CIP projects to its appropriate CIP accounts and to reconcile
GL balances with FS Schedule; and

b. Require the CAO and the City Engineering Office to work back and
reconcile their records and ascertain status of the ongoing and completed
projects of the LGU especially the dormant CIP accounts and the OIC-
City Accountant to make the necessary adjustment, if there is any.

We recommended that Management require:

a. The OIC-City Accountant and the City Treasurer to conduct a thorough
review of the unverified/unreconciled deposits and withdrawals from prior
years and submit the revised BRS along with its supporting documents, such
as deposit slips and details of these book and bank reconciling items to the
Office of the Auditor;

b. Make the necessary adjustments for valid reconciling items;

c. The OIC-City Accountant to record and maintain SLs for the unrecorded
bank accounts, including DBP (e-Gov), DBP (STF), and LP (BIR);

d. Submit the DVs and supporting documents that have not yet been submitted
for proper recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the aforementioned AOs, by issuing demand letters, to
deposit/turnover immediately their undeposited/unremitted collections
and submit copies of the RCDs, RCRs and deposit slips to the Office of the
Auditor. Henceforth, all subsequent collections must be remitted and
deposited intact with no portion retained by the AOs; and

b. Direct the AOs with negative/abnormal balances to coordinate with the
CAO and conduct immediate reconciliation of their respective records.
After which, the OIC-City Accountant must prepare the necessary
correcting entries in the Agency’s books to present fairly the Cash-Local
Treasury account in the FS.

We recommended that Management require:

a. The Inventory Committee to conduct a periodic physical count of all
inventory items in their possession;

b. The Property/Supply Officer to prepare and submit duly accomplished
RPCI;

c. The OIC-City Accountant to properly record the purchases of inventories
for consumption or distribution in the appropriate inventory accounts and
the consumption or distribution thereof as expenses upon receipt of SSMI
of a given period;

d. The CAO to maintain SLCs as required; and

e. Both the OIC-City Accountant and the Supply Officer to conduct periodic
reconciliation of their records and make adjustments, when necessary, to
reflect the proper balance of the Inventory Accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. Serve demand letters to all AOs with unliquidated CAs, not only to those
still connected with the LGU but also to those who have retired, resigned,
and transferred to other office/s; and

b. Require the OIC-City Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of
outstanding CAs, stop the practice of granting additional CAs to those who
have unliquidated CAs and include the “Certification that previous cash
advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books” as part of the
documentary requirements for CAs except for travels.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the City Budget Officer to ensure that all unpaid transactions are
obligated in his records and to regularly coordinate with the OIC-City
Accountant on the status of these obligations;

b. Instruct the City Treasurer to verify the claim and refrain from certifying
the availability of funds for disbursements without DVs and incomplete
supporting documents. If funds are unavailable to settle the obligations,
immediately return the DVs and their supporting documents to the OIC-
City Accountant for recording as AP;

c. Require the OIC-City Accountant to verify the completeness of DVs and
their supporting documents before recording in the books as AP, if the said
DVs are returned from the City Treasurer’s Office in accordance with
Section 44 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and

d. Direct the OIC-City Accountant to verify and trace back the disbursement
transactions which were recorded as AP to avoid payment which were not
among those listed, as well as payments that differ with the recorded AP
and if any, prepare necessary adjusting entry.

We recommended that Management:

a. Institute the filing of a request for authority to write-off and/or adjust the
dormant or non-moving accounts, duly supported by the required
documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005; and

b. Instruct the OIC-City Accountant to:

i. Thoroughly examine the entries made to the unreconciled
beginning balance of Due to NGAs account, including those which
caused the existence of abnormal balances in the prior years and
the net increase in CY 2024, to arrive at the accurate balance of the
account. After which, provide appropriate disclosure such as the
names of the concerned NGAs and the purposes of the fund
transfer/s;
ii. Prepare and submit liquidation reports of outstanding fund
transfers whose purposes were already completed, return any
unspent amount, and henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions
of Section 4 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13,
1994; and
iii. Reclassify the funds transfers amounting to ₱8,009,944.72 to proper
accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. Direct the OIC-City Accountant to prepare adjusting entries during the
ensuing year to reflect the accurate balances of Cash in Bank, Trust
Liability-DRRM and Prior Period Adjustments accounts, to wit:
GF TF
1. Reversion of CYs 2022 and 2023 Capital Outlay from TF to GF
Dr: Cash in Bank-LCCA ₱247,746.98 Dr: Trust Liability-DRRM ₱247,746.98
Cr: Prior Period Adjustment 247,746.98 Cr: Cash in Bank-LCCA 247,746.98
2. Transfer of Unobligated Balance of QRF and MOOE in CY 2024
Dr: Prior Period Adjustment ₱15,911,367.30 Dr: Cash in Bank-LCCA ₱15,911,367.30
Cr: Cash in Bank-LCCA 15,911,367.30 Cr: Trust Liability-DRRM 15,911,367.30

b. Direct the concerned officials to initiate the actual cash reversion of CY
2024 MF-Capital Outlay and CY 2023 Continuing Appropriations
amounting to ₱1,326,113.10 from the STF account to the GF account and
refrain from transferring any savings from LDRRMF MF-Capital Outlay
to the STF in the succeeding years.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

a. The CAO to install control measures on the observance of the required
timelines in the submission of accounts/reports to the Office of the Auditor,
more particularly on the GF-Proper accounts;

b. The City Treasurer to immediately submit the paid vouchers/payrolls
accounts and issued ORs on or before the 5th day of the ensuing month, to
the CAO for recording in the Agency books of accounts; and

c. The CAO to submit to the Auditor within the first 10 days of the ensuing
month, the paid vouchers/payrolls accounts and issued ORs including their
supporting documents pursuant to Section 107 of PD No. 1445 and COA
Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that Management require the OIC-City Accountant to:

a. Coordinate with the City Treasurer to facilitate the remittance of the
balances payable to BIR, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth to avoid the incurrence
of additional expenses in the form of surcharges/interests or penalties; and

b. Verify the entries made in the Due to GSIS account and conduct
reconciliation with GSIS to validate the abnormal/negative balances of the
account and make necessary adjustments, if there are any. If there are
over-remittances, a deduction from the salary of the concerned employee/s
must be made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:

a. Instruct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to diligently coordinate with
the officials concerned to ensure that the GAD PPAs in the GPB are
optimally, efficiently, and promptly implemented in order to achieve the
desired gender-responsiveness development of the LGU; and

b. Require the GFPS to submit an updated/revised GPB to the concerned
DILG Office for information and monitoring of the revised PPAs.

We recommended that Management strictly follow the provisions of the
Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed
under COA Circular No. 77-61, such as:

a. Require all drivers to accomplish the driver’s trip tickets with all
information required, among others, the plate number of the government
vehicle used, the purpose of travel, places to be visited, the approximate
distance from the place of origin and the odometer readings at the
beginning and end of the trips; and

b. Prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption (See Annex J for the
template) indicating the type of motor vehicles utilized by the Agency, total
distance traveled, total fuel used, and the normal fuel consumption for each
vehicle.

We also recommended that Management ensure complete and proper
documentation on the procurement of fuel, oil, and lubricants and minimize the
processing of reimbursements for fuel only in exceptional cases or in the exigency of
service, duly supported with accomplished driver’s trip tickets.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the concerned officers to prepare the prescribed Quarterly Report
on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation of
LGSF-SBDP and thereafter post the same in at least three conspicuous
places within the locality;

b. Post the aforementioned quarterly reports in the LGU’s website and notify
in writing the NTF-ELCAC Vice Chairperson and RTF-ELCAC
concerned, Speaker of the House of Representatives, President of the
Senate of the Philippines, and the Chairpersons of the House Committee
on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance when the reports are
already posted thereon; and

c. Submit the printout copies or any proof of posting of NOA, NTP and
contract in the PhilGEPS, LGU’s website and any conspicuous places.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. Strengthen the coordination among the City Engineering Office, CAO, and
City Treasurer’s Office to reconcile their respective records on the total
collection of building permits and other charges for CY 2024 and prior
years in order to determine the appropriate amount of the five percent
remittance to the DPWH and to reflect in the books of accounts the correct
balances of the affected accounts. Thereafter, immediately remit to the
NBCDO any discrepancies found in the reconciliation; and

b. Direct the concerned personnel to inform the NBCDO of its remittance on
April 30, 2025 and December 15, 2023. Also, verify from the BTr the nature
of the remittance in January 2023 on the Share of Bureau of Fire
(Buildings).

We recommended that Management continue to exert efforts to increase its
efficiency in the implementation of PPAs and utilization of 20 percent development
funds to achieve the CGM’s desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the OIC-City Accountant to strengthen its internal control in the pre-
audit of financial transactions and ensure that the supporting documents
are complete and accurate, otherwise, refrain from processing the claims or
payments of the same; and

b. Require the contractors to provide warranty securities after completion and
final acceptance of infrastructure projects pursuant to Section 62.2.3 of the
Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Inventory Committee to continuously make utmost effort to
conduct a complete and detailed physical inventory of all the
Municipality’s properties in accordance with the guidelines and
procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 and submit the
RPCPPE with complete details as required by NGAS for LGUs, Volume
II;

b. Require the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PCs
and PPELCs, respectively, per category on all PPE as recorded in the
books of accounts, and continuously coordinate with each other in order to
reconcile the discrepancies in the respective PPE accounts;

c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to depreciate all PPEs annually in
accordance with the regulations in order to reflect the accurate and
reliable value of PPE in the FS; and

d. Make necessary adjustments for the noted erroneous recording of
accounts, particularly those tangible items which meet the definition and
recognition criteria of PPE but cost is below ₱50,000.00. These items shall
be accounted in the books of accounts as semi-expendable property.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the immediate settlement/liquidation of the outstanding CAs
especially from all AOs and employees still connected with the LGU
through issuance of demand letters to be signed by the Honorable Mayor
or his authorized representative;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the grant,
utilization and liquidation of CAs and avoid granting additional CA unless
the previous CA is first settled and a proper accounting thereof is made;

c. Initiate the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring
AOs/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules
and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of CAs;

d. File a request to COA for authority to write-off and/or adjust the dormant
or non-moving accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005. The
request must be duly supported with the documents required in the said
circular; and

e. Submit the lacking documents related to the aforementioned CAs. Ensure
that all CAs are fully supported with the required documentation.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare and
submit the required monthly BRS for the month of December 2024, in
compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996, and to
substantiate the unreconciled difference amounting to ₱26,676,145.97; and

b. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare SLs for all bank
accounts.

We recommended that Management require:

a. The GSO to prepare and submit duly accomplished RPCI within the
prescribed period;

b. The GSO to consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials
were issued using the SSMI and submit these documents to Accounting
Unit for recording of expenses in the book of accounts;

c. The OIC-Municipal Accountant to discontinue recording supplies for stock
as outright expenses. Instead, purchases of inventories for consumption or
distribution should be properly recorded under the appropriate inventory
accounts, with the corresponding consumption or distribution recognized
as an expense upon receipt of the SSMI for a given period;

d. The Accounting Unit to maintain SLCs for each type of supplies to record
all receipts and issuances; and

e. Both the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to conduct periodic
reconciliation of their records and make adjustment, when necessary, to
reflect the true balance of the Inventory Accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough tracing
and analysis of the transactions recorded under the intra-agency accounts Due from
Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the identified discrepancies, and
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to ensure the accuracy and fair presentation
of the FS.

We recommended that Management:

a. Ensure the full and timely remittance of all amounts withheld from
employees to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth to avoid the accrual of
interest or penalties due to delayed remittances;

b. Identify and reconcile unremitted balances from prior years by
coordinating with GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth. Thereafter, promptly
remit the reconciled amounts to the respective agencies and record the
necessary adjustments in the books of accounts; and

c. Ensure that all material obligations existing at year-end are timely
recognized and accrued in accordance with IPSAS to avoid misstatements
in the FS.

We recommended that Management:

a. Submit the lacking supporting documents for the aforementioned
financial transactions;

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen its internal control in the
pre-audit of financial transactions and ensure that supporting documents
were complete, accurate, and duly approved by proper officials, in
compliance with Section 4 PD No. 1445;

c. Consider creating a checklist of documentary requirements for progress
and final billings to ensure that all supporting documents are complete
before processing payments, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-
001 and other procurement related regulations;

d. Require the Municipal Engineer to submit a certification for each project
(enumerated in Annex A), stating that it is on schedule and satisfactorily
undertaken, as a condition for the release of retention money before the
end of the defects liability period; and

e. Submit explanations on the noted deficiencies.

We recommended that Management:

a. Ensure that the POs were properly filled out with all the information/data
required as prescribed in the guidelines;

b. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to institute adequate internal
control such as thorough examination on the supporting documents and
other measures as deemed necessary and applicable before payment is
made;

c. Adhere strictly to the proper and complete documentary requirements
relative to the procurement of goods stated under COA Circular No. 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012; and

d. Submit the lacking supporting documents on the aforementioned
transactions.

We recommended that Management:

a. Ensure that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
immediately release the amount due to province and to various barangays
of the Municipality;

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the dormant balances of
Due to LGU account and properly dispose of these amounts in accordance
with the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular 2023-008; and

c. Require strict adherence to the guidelines relative to the prompt release of
the barangay shares from realty taxes stated under Section 271 (d) of the
Local Government Code in the ensuing years.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
ensure that the unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE be
transferred to STF at the end of the year;

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and
MDRRMO to conduct annual reconciliation of their respective records on
the utilization of LDRRMF to avoid inconsistent balances on their reports
and ensure that discrepancies noted are immediately investigated and
adjusted thereon accordingly; and

c. Consider incorporating the unexpended balance of LDRRMF from prior
years into the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the Municipality’s plan on
disaster preparedness and resilience is in accordance with RA No. 10121
and related guidelines.

We recommended that Management:

a. Formulate a uniform policy and procedures in the engagement of
contractual workers to ensure that the hiring process is free from bias and
has a consistent basis for assigning wage rates to improve the existing
system;

b. Engage the HRMO in the hiring process of contractual personnel to ensure
that candidates possess the necessary competencies and qualifications
required for their assigned roles; and

c. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking payrolls and
their corresponding supporting documents, and to exercise due diligence in
verifying the completeness and accuracy of all supporting documents prior
to the processing and release of payments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Office of the
Municipal Accountant to install control measures on the observance of the required
timelines in the submission of accounts/reports to the Office of the Auditor more
particularly on the GF-Proper accounts. Further, require the Office of the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately submit the paid vouchers/payrolls accounts and issued
official receipts (ORs) to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for recording in the
Agency’s book of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor
within the first 10 days of the ensuing month including their supporting documents
pursuant to Section 107 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006.

It is suggested that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer submit the monthly
accounts on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month to the Office of the
Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with the required deadlines.

We recommended that Management:

a. Reconsider incorporating the listed PPAs into other funds aside from
GAD Fund, such as GF, LDRRMF, and other special purpose fund;

b. Submit the DILG-endorsed GPB, GAD AR, and the corresponding
supporting documents for CY 2024, as required under PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, including the lacking supporting documents for
the related DVs; and

c. Submit explanation on the noted deficiencies.

We recommended the following:
a. Require the immediate settlement/liquidation of the outstanding CAs
especially from all AOs and employees still connected with the LGU
through issuance of demand letters to be signed by the Honorable Mayor
or his authorized representative;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the grant,
utilization and liquidation of CAs and avoid granting additional CA
unless the previous CA is first settled and a proper accounting thereof is
made;
c. Initiate the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring
AOs/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws,
rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of CAs;
d. File a request to COA for authority to write-off and/or adjust the
dormant or non-moving accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No.
2016-005. The request must be duly supported with the documents
required in the said circular; and
e. Submit the lacking documents related to the aforementioned CAs.
Ensure that all CAs are fully supported with the required
documentation.

We recommended that Management:
a. Reconstitute or reactivate the Inventory Committee through the
issuance of an executive order. Subsequently, direct them to prepare the
PIP for approval of the Local Chief Executive and submit the same to
the Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of
inventory-taking activities;
b. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all PPE
following the guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and
submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team;
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate to reconcile
their respective PPE records;
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all efforts to fully identify
the PPE with lump sum balances and at the same time secure the
documents to reconcile them with the Inventory Report of the GSO;
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to continue tracing back prior years’
acquisitions of PPE to have a complete detail of the PPE accounts so that
the corresponding depreciation could be computed, to present fairly the
carrying value of the depreciable PPE; and
f. Require the GSO–Designate and the Municipal Accounting Office to
avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, strictly in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly on the
disposition and derecognition of non-existing/missing PPE and adjust
the books accordingly, in order to come up with reliable PPE balances.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to give utmost attention to the
preparation and timely submission of the required monthly BRS, in
compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996, and
to substantiate the unreconciled difference amounting to ₱70,565,846.62;
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain updated cash
books and ensure these are regularly reconciled with the accounting
records;
c. Consider closing bank account/s which are deemed unnecessary or a
duplication of an existing account; and
d. Consider employing additional personnel in the Accounting Office to
support the timely preparation of BRS and the proper maintenance of
SLs.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendations that Management require:
a. The GSO-Designate to prepare and submit duly accomplished RPCI;
b. The Municipal Accountant to discontinue recording supplies for stock as
outright expenses. Instead, purchases of inventories for consumption or
distribution should be properly recorded under the appropriate
inventory accounts, with the corresponding consumption or distribution
recognized as an expense upon receipt of the SSMI for a given period;
c. The GSO-Designate to consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and
materials were issued using the SSMI and submit these documents to
Accounting Unit for recording of expenses in the book of accounts; and
d. The Accounting Unit to maintain SLCs and the GSO-Designate to
maintain SCs for each type of supplies to record all receipts and
issuances.

We recommended that Management:
a. Advise the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to
ascertain that all charges and payments under the LDRRMF align with
the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management and
strictly adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC Nos.
2013-1 and 2014-1;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the unexpended
balance of QRF and MF-MOOE be transferred to STF at the end of the
year;
c. Direct the MDRRMO to prepare the monthly report on utilization of
LDRRMF in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and
MDRRMO to conduct annual reconciliation of their respective records
on the utilization of LDRRMF to avoid inconsistent balances on their
reports and ensure that discrepancies noted are immediately
investigated and adjusted thereon accordingly;
e. Submit the lacking and appropriate supporting documents on the
aforementioned financial transactions; and
f. Submit justification on the noted deficiencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to
prioritize the recruitment process for the vacant position of LDRRM
Officer II to ensure sufficient staffing and continued support for the
effective operation of the LDRRMO; and
b. Direct the MDRRMO and the HRMO to initiate the creation of
additional positions within the LDRRMO and ensure these are filled
with qualified and well-trained personnel, in order to properly
institutionalize the LDRRMO in accordance with the provisions of
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

We recommended that Management:
a. Exert effort in locating the pertinent documents of the loan from Asian
Development Bank in order to determine the correct outstanding loan
balance as of December 31, 2024;
b. Make the necessary adjusting entry/ies to correct the recorded balance
of the LGU’s outstanding Loans Payable account as of year-end;
c. Properly classify the completed construction of wharf project as Seaport
Systems instead of Other Infrastructure Assets; and
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly dispose the balance of LBP
Loan which has been dormant for more than 10 years using the
guidelines set in COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended that Management:
a. Submit the lacking supporting documents, along with those DVs that
were not approved by the proper officials for the aforementioned
financial transactions;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen its internal control in the
pre-audit of financial transactions and ensure that supporting
documents were complete, accurate, and duly approved by proper
officials, in compliance with Sections 4 (5) and (6) PD No. 1445; and
c. Consider creating a checklist of documentary requirements for progress
and final billings to ensure that all supporting documents are complete
before processing payments, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-
001 and other procurement related regulations;
d. Deduct retention money from every progress billing due to contractor or
require the contractor to secure irrevocable standby letters of credit,
bank guarantees and/or surety bonds, to cover uncorrected discovered
defects and third-party liabilities in accordance and compliance with the
2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
e. Submit explanations on the noted deficiencies.

We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure that the POs were properly filled out with all the
information/data required as prescribed in the guidelines;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to institute adequate internal control
such as thorough examination on the supporting documents and other
measures as deemed necessary and applicable before payment is made;
c. Adhere strictly to the proper and complete documentary requirements
relative to the procurement of goods stated under COA Circular No. 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012; and
d. Submit the lacking supporting documents on the aforementioned
transactions.

We recommended that Management:
a. Submit immediately the lacking supporting documents for the aforecited
transactions;
b. Designate an officer responsible for conducting pre-repair and post-repair
evaluations/inspections of all government vehicles subjected to repairs; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all future payments for
the repair and maintenance of vehicles are supported with complete and
appropriate documents, in accordance with Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular
No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the HRMO to formulate a uniform policy and procedures in the
engagement of contractual workers to ensure that the hiring process is
free from bias and has a consistent basis for assigning wage rates to
improve the existing system;
b. Engage the HRMO in the hiring process of contractual personnel to
ensure that candidates possess the necessary competencies and
qualifications required for their assigned roles;
c. Delegate to each Department Head the responsibility of preparing the
payroll for their respective JO personnel, ensuring that it is duly
supported with accomplished DTRs and accomplishment reports;
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting
documents and to exercise due diligence in verifying the completeness
and accuracy of all supporting documents prior to the processing and
release of payments; and
e. Submit justification on the noted deficiencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough tracing
and analysis of the transactions recorded under the intra-agency accounts Due
from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the identified
discrepancies, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to ensure the
accuracy and fair presentation of the FS.

We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure the full and timely remittance of all amounts withheld from
employees to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth to avoid the accrual of
interest or penalties due to delayed remittances; and
b. Identify and reconcile unremitted balances from prior years, including
abnormal balances, by coordinating with GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and
PhilHealth. Thereafter, promptly remit the reconciled amounts to the
respective agencies and record the necessary adjustments in the books of
accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned Special
Disbursing Officers to immediately submit their liquidation reports for
the months of April and December 2024 to the Municipal Accounting
Office;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the missing DVs
and RDs, along with complete supporting documents; and
c. Hire additional competent staffs for the Accounting Office to expedite
the processing and recording of voluminous financial transactions of the
Municipality and ensure timely submission of all accounts within the
prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as Chairman of the LSB, to:
a. Direct the LSB to ensure the timely review and approval of the SEF
Budget not later than October 31 of the preceding year, in accordance
with prescribed guidelines, to facilitate proper planning and potential
budget augmentation;
b. Require allocation of a specific portion of the SEF for the ECCD
Program in compliance with Item 3.4 of the JC and Section 7(b) of RA
No. 10410 to support early childhood development initiatives;
c. Direct the LSB to formulate a three-year program indicating strategic
prioritization policies in the allocation of the SEF to schools prior to the
formulation and preparation of its Annual Budget for the incoming year;
d. Require the submission of the quarterly and annual reports using the
SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF utilization, as
prepared by the Local Accountant, to the concerned offices—DepEd
Central Office (through appropriate channels), Local Sanggunian, and
regional offices of DBM and DILG;
e. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
submit certified statements regarding the actual SEF income (for the
immediately preceding fiscal year and for the first two quarters of the
current year) and the estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of
71
the current year not later than July 15 of the current year; and
f. Submit explanation/justification on the aforementioned deficiencies.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Point System
(GFPS) to:
a. Establish and maintain a complete GAD database to provide accurate
information and bases for an effective gender planning, programming
and policy formulation and utilized the GAD Fund only for acceptable
PPAs that would address the identified gender issues in the locality based
on RA No. 9710 or the MCW, PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-
001 and other relevant international commitments;
b. Reconsider incorporating the listed PPAs into other funds aside from
GAD Fund, such as GF, LDRRMF, and other special purpose fund;
c. Submit the GAD AR and its attachments for CY 2024 as mentioned in
Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 and Section 4.C.8.5 of PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
d. Submit the lacking supporting documents of the aforementioned
transactions as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 and PD No.
1445; and
e. Submit explanation on the noted deficiencies.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct annual inventory-taking of all
PPE and submit the RPCPPE in the prescribed form based on the actual
physical count conducted, together with the RPCLRN of the LGU within the
prescribed period;

b. Require the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to prepare and
maintain the property records for all PPE and undertake the required
reconciliation of the Property and Accounting records thereafter;

c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to: i) ensure monthly recognition of
depreciation expense of all PPE accounts and record the adjustments in the
government equity account for the prior year’s depreciation expenses; ii)
deduct a residual value equivalent to at least five percent of the cost before
the computation of depreciation expense; iii) ensure that PPE are recorded
at their total acquisition cost rather than on a per payment basis; iv)
immediately transfer the completed PPE recorded in the TF to the GF; and
v) make the necessary adjusting entries in the books to correct the identified
misclassified PPE to come up with the correct PPE accounts balances; and

d. Facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the
implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the LCE:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) give preferential attention in the
preparation and submission of the required BRS for all accounts; (ii)
maintain an updated SL for each bank account; (iii) prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to account for the stale checks; and (iv) adjust the long-
outstanding reconciling items in the books to correct the amount presented
in the FS and other documents; and

b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) consistently update all cashbooks to
ensure that the data reflected therein is accurate and can be reliably used in
the preparation of a complete BRS; and (ii) promptly cancel stale checks
and initiate the replacement process in accordance with Section 59 of the
Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all
supplies and materials on hand including the semi-expendable properties,
submit the required RPCI and RPCSP and coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the same with the accounting records;

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchase of
inventories in the appropriate inventory accounts and the issuance,
consumption and distribution thereof as expense upon receipt of RIS and
SSMI of a given period and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the
correct balance of the affected inventory and government equity accounts; and

c. Require the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to properly
maintain, update and reconcile the SCs and SLCs, respectively. Also,
strengthen controls over the semi-expendable properties by establishing and
maintaining accurate records, reports, and registries as required by COA
Circular No. 2022-004.

We recommended that Management:

a. Prioritize issuance of demand letters to all AOs and employees to
immediately liquidate/settle their outstanding cash advances, especially
from those who are still connected with the LGU;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the grant and
liquidation of cash advances and avoid granting additional cash advance
unless the previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting
thereof is made, strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring
controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and avoid
undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances;

c. Fast track the recovery of the documents relevant to the long outstanding
cash advances and conduct verification, analysis, make necessary
adjusting entries and validation to determine the settlement/collectability
and pursue collection, refund or liquidation, if warranted; and

d. Conform with the procedures and requirements set forth in COA Circular
No. 2016-005 and immediately facilitate the request for write-off of dormant
unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the LCE:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer and demand from the Former Municipal
Treasurers to deposit immediately all their undeposited collections and
submit a copy of the deposit slip to the Office of the Auditor. Otherwise,
institute appropriate administrative charges against the Municipal
Treasurer/s for inability to deposit their collections within the period
required by law and regulations;

b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the updated
cashbooks to the Office of the Auditor. Henceforth, regularly update her
cashbooks and ensure regular reconciliation of the same with the records of
the Municipal Accountant; and

c. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the collectors to deposit/turnover all
future collections intact and not to retain portion of them pursuant to the
aforementioned provisions.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accounting Office
and the Municipal Engineering Office to reconcile their records as to the status and
costs incurred related to the ongoing and completed projects of the Municipality.
Thereafter, require the Municipal Accountant to close the CIP accounts to its
appropriate PPE accounts and make proper disclosures in the Notes to FS of the
circumstances of the balances.

We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: (i) issue immediately the checks for
each LGU at an amount equivalent to their respective RPT shares; and (ii)
henceforth, ensure the prompt and regular remittance of the shares of
concerned LGUs within five days after the end of each quarter; and

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the necessary adjusting
journal entries to reclassify the Transfer from TF-Proper from the Due to
LGUs account of the GF to the Trust Liability account of TF for fair and
accurate presentation of account balances in the FS; and (ii) consistently
notify the LGUs of their respective RPT share amounts each quarter to
enable them to claim the same from the Treasury Office.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all the
DVs, liquidation vouchers and issued accountable forms including the
corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the
ensuing month;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the
paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month and submit
immediately to the Office of the Auditor the 25 lacking DVs complete with
supporting documents; and

c. Remind the concerned officials that processing of vouchers be only
undertaken to those which were properly approved/signed and with
complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to
COA all the aforementioned cancelled and unused old checks for validation and
henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported
chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “Cancelled” clearly
indicated therein. Likewise, prudence must be exercised in encoding the details
of the checks in order to avoid wastage of checks/resources.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the LSB to ensure that prescribed planning and budgeting tools are
prepared, submitted and effectively utilized;

b. Ensure that the timelines in the planning and budgeting process are strictly
observed as prescribed under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017;

c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that proper accounts are used in
the recording of the financial transactions and that the supporting documents
are proper and complete before processing the claim or payment of the same;
and

d. Instruct the BAC to strictly abide with the guidelines on the use of
appropriate alternative mode of procurement.

We recommended that Management amend its local ordinance with regards to
AICS to ensure strict adherence to the guidelines stipulated in DSWD MC No. 16, s.
2022, as amended by DSWD MC No. 6, s. 2023, thereby promoting regulatory
compliance and consistency in the provision of aid.

We recommended that Management:

a. Conduct an in-depth verification of the Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and
PhilHealth accounts, perform reconciliation between the SLs and the
Schedules of Remittances, make necessary adjustments in the books to
reflect accurate balances and remit immediately all overdue obligations to
prevent further accumulation and avoid potential penalties, if warranted,
and;

b. Evaluate the current system of withholding and remitting taxes, premiums,
contributions and other deductions to prevent under or over remittance
thereof.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the amount of
₱52,500,000.00 to the TF – Proper account;

b. Fast track the implementation of the subject projects and evaluate whether
those projects which have not yet started are still feasible, otherwise, return
the unutilized fund to the BTr; and

c. Prepare and submit the following:

i. Supplemental LDIP and AIP which include the projects funded by the
LGSF and Unprogrammed Appropriations - SBDP NTF-ELCAC;

ii. Quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project
Implementation using the prescribed format; and

iii. Separate SLs for each beneficiary barangay and projects to be
implemented.

We recommended that Management:

a. Submit a written explanation on the deficiencies noted in the submitted
documents;

b. Direct the BAC to exercise stricter scrutiny in the preparation and
evaluation of bidding documents, conduct of procurement activities and
ensure compliance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other relevant
procurement guidelines. Further, require the submission of complete and
accurate documentary requirements on procurement transactions pursuant
to COA Circular No. 2012-001;

c. Require the posting of warranty security as one of the documentary
requirements for final payment and ensure that its validity period is in
accordance with Section 62.2.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and

d. Comply with the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, particularly by
ensuring that the development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent
DF are well-planned and procurement and implementation-ready.

We recommended that the LCE:

a. In coordination with the HRMO-Designate, prepare a staff pattern plan for
non-permanent employees and review and evaluate the existing manpower
resources to ensure adequate staffing pattern that suit the present needs of
the offices and create clear-cut policies and guidelines to address the issue
on the necessity and propriety of hiring job orders/holders of COS;

b. Direct the Heads of the Municipal Offices to review the DTRs and ARs of
the job orders and/or contract of service personnel under their respective
offices before approving the same to ensure that pertinent supporting
documents were duly accomplished and signed by the employees
concerned; and

c. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking
supporting documents and henceforth, ensure that all payments to job
orders and casual employees are properly supported with complete
documentation.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to exert effort in
identifying the details of unreconciled prior balances, update their
respective books and records accordingly and reconcile any discrepancies
thereafter;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to prepare the Report
on Utilization of the LDRRMF in an itemized format to ensure that each
expenditure is clearly linked to its corresponding PPA in the LDRRMFIP;

c. Stop the incurrence of non-allowable administrative and operating charges
and other office expenditures in the LDRRMF. Also, require the MDRRMO to
submit a justification/legal basis to support disbursements of the LDRRMF
amounting to ₱1,063,464.22; and

d. Enjoin the LDRRM Office to closely monitor the implementation of all PPAs
under the LDRRMF, including those under the STF to facilitate corrective
action, if any, and the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for
its optimum utilization.

We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO)/
GFPS Focal Person, in coordination with the MPDC, to facilitate the
gathering of data and information needed for the creation of an updated and
well-established GAD database to have an accurate and reliable basis in the
planning and programming of PPAs as well as in the policy formulation in
order to properly address the existing and emerging GAD issues and
concerns;

b. Direct the GFPS and GAD key personnel to undergo capacity building
programs and training on gender and development to enhance their
knowledge and build their competencies on GAD-related laws and
international commitments, gender mainstreaming, gender analysis,
gender-responsive planning and budgeting, and GAD-related tools like
HGDG on the attributed budget to evaluate gender responsiveness of PPAs
in the GAD Plan and Budget; and

c. Ensure that the PPAs charged to GAD fund are only those that would
address the identified gender issues in the locality. The gender issues must
be specific and supported by evidence such as sex-disaggregated data and
gender analysis results.

We recommended that Management require the:

a. Inventory Committee to continuously make utmost effort to conduct a
complete and detailed physical inventory of all the LGU’s properties in
accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed by COA Circular
No. 2020-006;

b. Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain complete and updated
PPELCs and PCs for all PPEs; and

c. Municipal Accountant and the GSO to make a final reconciliation of their
respective records and avail the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account
balances, including the disposition and derecognition of non-
existing/missing PPE and those without available records of accountability
and adjust the books accordingly, in order to come up with reliable PPE
balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal
Accountant to: a) prepare and submit the required BRS pursuant to Section 3 of COA
Circular No. 96-011, b) maintain SLs per bank accounts c) review the records relevant
to the Cash in Bank account and effect necessary adjustments to correct the amount
presented in the FS and other documents.

We recommended that Management:

a. Exhaust all possible means to require the concerned officers and employees
to liquidate/settle immediately their outstanding CAs by serving demand
letters to AOs, not only to those still connected with the LGU but also to
those who retired, resigned and transferred to other office/s, to immediately
submit their liquidation papers or settle their accountabilities with the
LGU; and

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of
outstanding CAs, refrain from granting additional CAs to those who have
unliquidated CAs, and enforce the use of "Certification that previous cash
advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books" as part of the
documentary requirements for CAs except for travels.

We recommended that Management require the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all inventory
items in their possession. Likewise, the Property/Supply Officer must
prepare and submit duly accomplished RPCI;

b. Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of inventories for
consumption or distribution in the appropriate inventory accounts and the
Municipal GSO to submit consolidated RIS and SSMI to the Accounting
Office for the preparation of JEV to record the expenditures using
appropriate expenditure accounts; and

c. Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain SLCs and SCs,
respectively, as required in both their offices. Both the Accountant and the
Supply Officer to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records and make
adjustment, when necessary, to reflect the true balance of the Inventory
Accounts in the FS.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the remittance of the
balances payable to BIR, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth to avoid the incurrence
of additional expenses in the form of surcharges/interests or penalties; and

b. Verify the entries made in the Due to GSIS account and conduct
reconciliation with the concerned Agency to validate the abnormal/negative
balances of the account and make necessary adjustments, if there are any. If
there is over-remittance, a deduction from the salary of the concerned
employee/s must be made.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit
the lacking check disbursements, cash disbursements and collection accounts with
complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. We also recommended
that the Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly trial balances and quarterly FS
and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management

a. Require the concerned officials to submit the required lacking supporting
documents;

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen its internal control in the
pre-audit of financial transactions and ensure that payments for advances,
progress billings, final payments, and release of retention money were
supported with complete and properly accomplished documentation,
otherwise refrain from processing the claims or payment of the same;

c. Require the BAC to exercise thorough scrutiny in the preparation of
bidding and other procurement activity documents and submit the
required copy of the Contract with complete supporting documents in
accordance with laws, rules and regulation in the procurement of
infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management:

a. Prioritize securing the appropriate supporting documents for the purchase
of lots as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 to support the claims
of ownership and to safeguard the rights of the Municipality, and to submit
the said documents to the Office of the Auditor as soon as possible;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor
the unsubmitted DVs and the supporting documents for review and
evaluation; and

c. Strengthen the internal control in the pre-audit of financial transactions to
ensure that the supporting documents are complete and properly
accomplished before processing the claim or payment of the same.

We recommended that the LCE:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen its internal control in the
pre-audit of financial transactions. Ensure that the supporting documents
were complete and properly accomplished/filled out as to details and
necessary information; otherwise, refrain from processing the claim or
payment of the same; and

b. Require the concerned personnel to submit the lacking documents and
rectify the deficiencies of the above-mentioned financial transactions in
order to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management:

a. Established guidelines on the utilization of Telephone Expenses-Mobile to
specify the personnel who are entitled and to limit load allowances or
expenses granted to officials and employees of the Municipality in
accordance with laws, rules and regulations; and

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents in
accordance with Section 6.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012.

We recommended that Management:

a. Limit the allocation and disbursement of funds for discretionary,
extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses within the rates prescribed in
Section 325 (h) of RA No. 7160;

b. Strengthen control on the disbursements of funds and refrain from paying
expenses that are not supported with complete and properly accomplished
documentation;

c. Exercise prudence in the utilization of funds. Ensure that allocation for
extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses is used only for the purpose for
which these were established. Refrain from charging to this account
expenses which must are covered by separate appropriations;

d. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer to reconcile their
records on allotments available for obligation at the end of each month; and

e. Submit the lacking supporting documents and explanation for the noted
deficiencies.

We recommend that Management:

a. Enjoin the LDRRMO to exert more effort in the implementation of PPAs
under the LDRRM to optimize its utilization and to help build a disaster-
resilient LGU and communities;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer
all the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF, except CO, from the GF to the
Trust Fund (TF) under the account Trust Liability – DRRM. Ensure that the
unexpended balance of QRF and Mitigation Fund (MF) – MOOE for each
year be timely transferred at year-end with the corresponding cash back up
in order to reflect the correct balance of the cash and liability accounts in
the FS;

c. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to revert
back to the unappropriated surplus of the GF unutilized amount in the
Special Trust Fund (STF) beyond five years;

d. Instruct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit within the prescribed
format the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF
with complete and sufficient information on time and facilitate monitoring
of the accomplishment vis-à-vis the targeted projects for effective and
efficient utilization of the fund; and

e. Direct the MDRRMO to prepare the annual LDRRMFIP detailing all the
PPAs to be funded, including the list of PPAs to be undertaken out of the
previous years’ unexpended balances under STF using the prescribed
Format in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GFPS to diligently coordinate with
the officials concerned to ensure that the GAD PPAs are optimally, efficiently and
promptly implemented to achieve the desired gender-responsiveness development of
the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the concerned officers to prepare the prescribed Quarterly Report on
Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation of LGSF-
SBDP and thereafter post the same in at least three conspicuous places
within the locality;

b. Likewise, post the aforementioned quarterly reports on the LGU’s website
and notify in writing the NTF-ELCAC Vice Chairperson and Regional Task
Force (RTF)-ELCAC concerned, Speaker of the House of Representatives,
President of the Senate of the Philippines, and the Chairpersons of the House
Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance when the
reports are already posted thereon; and

c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the
Quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project
Implementation of LGSF-SBDP, together with the proof of its posting within
the locality, as well as the received notification letters to the above-
mentioned offices.

We recommended that Management:
Reconstitute or reactivate the Inventory Committee through the issuance of an Executive Order. Subsequently, direct them to prepare the PIP for approval of the Chief Executive and submit the same to the Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all PPE following the guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team; Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO - Designate to reconcile their respective PPE records; Direct the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPE with lump sum balances and at the same time secure the documents to reconcile them with the Inventory Report of the GSO; Direct the Municipal Accountant to continue tracing back prior years’ acquisitions of PPE to have a complete detail of the PPE accounts so that the corresponding depreciation could be computed, to present fairly the carrying value of the depreciable PPE; Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify to Seaport Systems account payment for the land expansion and port development of Mobo amounting to ₱2,996,269.52 erroneously recorded as Land and Other Structures accounts, respectively; and Require the GSO - Designate and the Municipal Accounting Office to avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, strictly in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly on the disposition and derecogntion of non-existing/missing PPE and adjust the books accordingly, in order to come up with a reliable PPE balances.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel that will solely focus on the conduct of existing inventory and reconciliation of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
i) Facilitate the immediate transfer of the completed project to its appropriate PPE account in accordance with Section 50 of the Manual on NGAS; ii) Work back to trace the entries of the dormant CIP accounts and ascertain the status of the projects recorded; iii) Make the necessary adjustments to correct the actual balance of the CIP accounts, PPE accounts, the corresponding Depreciation Expense accounts and other related accounts affected by the non-transfer of completed projects and errors that could have been committed in the recording of the CIP; and iv) Recognize construction in progress based on progress billings and other acceptable and appropriate supporting documents which will properly substantiate its balance. The Municipal Engineer to constantly update the Municipal Accountant on the status of accomplishments of on-going Municipality’s projects for accurate presentation of related accounts in the FS.
observations.managementAction: a. Make adjusting entries to transfer completed projects to its PPE account. b. Identify and trace back all the records of continuing and prior year completed projects. c. Prepare necessary correcting and adjusting journal entries. d. The accounting will recognize CIP based on progress billings. e. The Municipal Engineering and Accounting Office will closely monitor all on-going and completed projects.
observations.reason: Lack of staff from Municipal Office and Assign personnel that will solely focus on the reconciliation of prior year accounts and monitor the ongoing projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Submit to the Audit Team copies of the MOA and supporting documents on the subject FTs and ensure their submission for any subsequent FTs; and Direct the Municipal Accountant to: i) Review, correct and update the identified schedules with negative balance and conduct periodical check for negative (abnormal) balances in the accounts and adjust accordingly; ii) Evaluate the non-moving sub-account balances and facilitate the necessary liquidation/refund of unexpended balance of those projects to the Source Agency (SA); iii) Submit to the concerned SA the liquidation reports and return/remit the outstanding balance, for completed projects, if warranted. If the disposition of the unspent/unutilized amount is not provided in the MOA, Trust Agreement or Memorandum of Understanding, the agency may, at their option, utilize the said amount by sending a letter to the SA requesting approval to transfer the unexpended balance to the GF; and iv) Reconcile the Due to NGAs account and take up the necessary correcting/adjusting entries for fair presentation in the FS.
observations.managementAction: a. The BAC will provide copies of approved contracts. b. Identify and trace back all abnormal balances in the accounts. c. Review, correct and update the identified schedules with negative balance and conduct periodical check for negative (abnormal) balances in the accounts and adjust accordingly; d. Evaluate the non-moving sub-account balances and facilitate the necessary liquidation/refund of unexpended balance of those projects to the SA; e. Submit liquidation to the concerned SA.
observations.reason: Lack of staff from Municipal Accounting Office that will solely focus on the records reconciliation and trace back records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the GSO and the Inventory Committee with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of the RPCLRN; Direct the GSO to prepare and submit the RLRN, in accordance with the prescribed formats as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008; and Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the total road network in the Notes to the FS and provide depreciation to all road networks following the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: a. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the GSO together with the Inventory Committee, if any, with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of the RPCLRN; b. Direct the GSO to prepare and submit the RLRN; c. The Municipal Accountant will conduct reconciliation of records together with the GSO and the Inventory Committee to properly disclose the total road networks and provide for its depreciation.
observations.reason: Non-organization of Inventory Committee that will focus on the reconciliation and conduct of inventory of existing road networks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Agency Head instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking/unsubmitted CY 2023 TBs, Financial Reports and Statements, DVs with complete supporting documents, collections accounts and other monthly reports as well as the lacking DVs from prior years. Otherwise, the subject disbursements will be disallowed in audit. Henceforth, require the Municipal Officials concerned to comply with the rules and regulations on the submission of paid DVs, Official Receipts, Journal Entry Vouchers, FS and other Financial Reports.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Head/Accountant to immediately submit the lacking/unsubmitted CY 2023 TBs, Financial Reports and Statements, DVs with complete supporting documents, collections accounts and other monthly reports as well as the lacking DVs from prior years within the prescribed period. Henceforth, require the Municipal Officials concerned to comply with the rules and regulations on the submission of paid DVs, ORs, JEVs, FS and other Financial Reports.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel that will focus on the completion of lacking and unsubmitted reports. The Accounting Office will assign employee solely for the submission of TBs and quarterly financial reports and its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Enjoin the MDRRMO to exert more effort in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk reduction and management towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and communities; Closely monitor the implementation of all PPAs under the LDRRMF to facilitate corrective action, if any, and the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization; Advise the Municipal Budget Officer to ascertain that all charges in the LDRRMF are related to four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management and strictly adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC Nos. 2013-1 and 2014-1; Create a special trust fund bank account for the unexpended DRRMF and upon creation, immediately draw a check from the GF to transfer the unexpended current and prior years’ LDRRMF balances to the TF books under Trust-Liability DRRM account. Likewise, ensure that the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE for each year be timely transferred at year-end with the corresponding cash back-up in order to reflect the correct balances of the cash and liability accounts in the FS; Require the MDRRMO to include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the preparation of LDRRMF Investment Plan; and Require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRMO to conduct annual reconciliation of their respective records on the utilization of LDRRMF to avoid inconsistent balances on their reports and ensure that discrepancies noted are immediately investigated and adjusted thereon accordingly.
observations.managementAction: a. Enjoin the MDRRMO to exert more effort in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk reduction and management towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and communities; b. Closely monitor the implementation of all PPAs under the LDRRMF to facilitate corrective action, if any, and the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization; c. Advise the Municipal Budget Officer to ascertain that all charges in the LDRRMF are related to four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management and strictly adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC Nos. 2013-1 and 2014-1; d. Create a special trust fund bank account for the unexpended DRRMF and upon creation, immediately draw a check from the GF to transfer the unexpended current and prior years’ LDRRMF balances to the TF books under Trust-Liability DRRM account; and e. Issue demand letters to officials and employees, who have outstanding CAs as of December 31, 2023, especially those who are still in the active service with material amount including those individuals who are assigned in other LGUs or other offices. A copy of which must be submitted to the Audit Team with proof of service and dated to enforce settlement of unliquidated CAs; and
Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the CAs especially those accounts with unspecified balances and prepare an adjustments, if necessary.
