observations.reason: Inability of the Office of the City Accountant to submit and monitor the Depreciation Expenses as well as Accumulated Depreciation of depreciable properties of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Office of the City Accountant to strictly maintain the required Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs). We would like to emphasize that the SLs must be reconciled to their respective general ledger control account balances. Moreover, we strongly recommended that the Office of the City Accountant evaluate the existing Subsidiary Ledgers to determine the cause of the abnormal balances thereof. Appropriate adjustment must be undertaken to be backed up by working papers or documents for the errors that may be discovered in the evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The SLs of some accounts were already maintained. However, SLs of some significant accounts, such as Accounts Receivables were not maintained.
observations.reason: Evaluation of the year end supporting schedules disclosed that there are still existing abnormal accounts and unreconciled beginning balances of SL accounts which does not tally with the general ledger controlling balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to immediately prepare and submit to COA the above-mentioned CRAAF for the calendar years 2009 to CY 2016 and every month thereafter. Likewise, require ICO, City Treasurer to submit the same report for the period from August 2016 to December 2016. In addition, require the CGSO to stop the practice of releasing additional accountable forms to the City Treasurer’s Office and Barangay Treasurers without requiring the submission of the copies of the Report of Collections and Deposits corresponding to the issuance of the previous ORs/Accountable Forms. See to it that proper coordination with the Accounting office is undertaken to ensure that all collections have been recorded in the books of city and barangays. Strict compliance with the provisions of Section 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II, is required.

We recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System must promptly prepare and submit the agency’s GAD Plan and  Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February  of each year to see to it that the GPB of the city is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB must be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. The certificate of approval from the latter will ensure that the programs/projects/activities reflected in the GPB are in accordance with the provisions of the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001 and other existing regulations/guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The agency was able to submit the GAD reports to PPDO & DILG for review. Please see related favorable Audit Observation for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Require the City Accountant to prepare a Disbursement Voucher for the transfer of the unutilized balance of ₱13,820,089.81 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM. Ensure that the unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE for each year be timely transferred at year-end with the corresponding cash back-up;
The Accounting Office to coordinate with the Budget Office to reconcile their records and obtain the correct amount of the unexpended balances; and
Require the City Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger of the Trust Liability-DRRMF account by source and by year when the savings were realize.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Office of the City Treasurer to immediately submit the paid vouchers/payrolls accounts and issued ORs to the Office of the City Accountant for recording in the agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing including their supporting documents pursuant to Section 107 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006.

It is suggested that the Office of the City Treasurer submit the monthly accounts on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month to the Office of the City Accountant to ensure compliance with the required deadlines. Prompt submission of accounts and other mandated reports will facilitate timely review and verification of the agency’s financial transactions, thus corrective measures can be implemented, if necessary.

The Management and the concerned officials and employees are also reminded of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445, Section 23 (1) (3) of the Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, which may be imposed as a consequence of their failure or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause.
observations.reason: Restated finding- Please see related Audit Observation No. 12.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Office of the City Treasurer to immediately submit the Disbursement Vouchers including their supporting documents for checks omitted from the Reports of Checks Issued (RCIs) to the Office of the City Accountant for recording in the agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the soonest possible time in compliance with Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445. Prompt submission of accounts and other mandated reports will facilitate timely review and verification of the agency’s financial transactions and appropriate audit action or corrective measures, if necessary, will be undertaken to prevent adverse audit actions. Moreover, it will enhance fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements since all actual disbursements will be accordingly recorded in the books for the period to which they have occurred.

We suggested that the Management evaluate their existing internal control system in the actual procedure of handling the check disbursement transactions particularly on the custodial functions/release of the disbursement vouchers with their complete supporting documents at the Office of the City Treasurer to ensure that all accounts/ documents are forwarded  to the Office of the City Accountant on the required deadline to prevent the further occurrence of this deficiency in the succeeding years.

The Management and the concerned officials/employees are also reminded of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, which may be imposed as a consequence of their inability or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause.
observations.managementAction: Policies and measures are now being considered that will strengthen the internal control system of the LGU. The Agency was able to minimize the missing/lacking DVs at the time of submission to the Auditor.
observations.reason: Remaining DVs are still under verification and validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned accountable officers to prepare and update their cashbooks and submit the same to the audit team for verification purposes. It is also reminded that cashbooks must be submitted to the accounting office for reconciliation purposes in order to arrive at the correct and accurate balances of cash accounts as of a specified period. In this regard, the ICO-City Treasurer monitor strict compliance by all accountable officers with the above-cited guidelines in the maintenance of cashbooks.
observations.managementAction: Some Accountable Officers (AOs) have complied the audit recommenda-tion.
observations.reason: Other AOs still unable to properly maintain and update cashbooks causing difficulty in the conduct of cash examination. Reconciliation with the cashbooks and accounting records of prior year’s accounts are still on going. Please see related Audit Observation No. 5.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Office of the City Accountant to immediately submit all Accountant’s Advice of Local Check Disbursements of the city government. See to it that copies of Accountant’s Advice in the ensuing period are regularly and completely submitted to the Audit Team as mandated in COA Circular No. 96-007 dated May 15, 1996. We suggested that it must be forwarded to the Auditor on a weekly basis for all funds per bank account check issuances.
observations.managementAction: The LGU was only able to comply for General Fund-Proper month of June 2019.
observations.reason: The submitted Accountant’s Advice were returned by the Auditor because of incomplete data. Restated audit finding. Please see related Audit Observation No. 13.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the ICO-City Treasurer and Liquidating Officer to ensure that all collections and issued accountable forms are closely reviewed, scrutinized and where necessary, recalculated in order to avoid incurrence of under collections/under remittances. Require the Collecting Clerks to remit the amount of audit charge. Strict adherence to the above-mentioned guidelines on collections and deposits is required.
observations.managementAction: The new OIC, City Treasurer has introduced measures to strengthen somehow the internal control in the treasury department operations.
observations.reason: The Audit Team observed some lapses in the reporting activities of some collectors as well as the liquidating officer in the treasury department.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to Sections 7, 8 and 9 of COA Circular No. 97-001 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 governing the rendition of accounts and submission of copies of contracts is required. Direct the BAC Secretariat, City Engineer and City Planning and Development Coordinator to closely coordinate in the compliance of the required documentation and submission to the Audit Team. Further, it is suggested that the Office of the City Accountant require the attachment of complete supporting documents on the duly processed disbursement vouchers pertaining to infrastructure projects before effecting the payment.
observations.reason: Please see related Audit Observation No. 12.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to record receivable account from the League to ensure fair presentation of the account.

Henceforth, for control purposes, ensure that the transfer is properly taken up in the books of both agencies, the transferred funds are used only for the intended purpose, and that proper accounting and reporting is made of the utilization of the funds.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant made necessary adjustments in compliance to COA recommenda-tions.
observations.reason: Securing of Official Receipts for the annual dues is underway.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Office of the City Treasurer conduct intensification of its revenue collection campaign and install appropriate monitoring mechanisms to ensure efficient and effective collection of targeted revenues.

We suggested that the Offices of the City Assessor and City Treasurer coordinate and install measures on how to strengthen the monitoring mechanisms relative to taxes due on real properties and the delinquent taxpayers. Moreover, strengthen the internal control system in the reporting and recording process of collections to eliminate errors and increase actual collections of revenues due for the city government.

We recommended and reiterated that Management:
Convene the Inventory Committee, conduct the annual physical count of all its property, plant and equipment and submit the corresponding Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor;
Evaluate the Agency’s property and supply management system, identify lapses therein and correct the same the soonest; and
Direct the City General Services Officer (CGSO)-Designate and City Accountant to maintain the respective complete property records required from their offices and reconcile their records.
Strict adherence to existing laws, rules and regulations in the proper management of government properties is required.

Management Comment:
Management was receptive of the audit observations and assured the Audit Team that they will exert their best efforts in order to fully comply with the requirements on property management.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to prepare the Adjusting Journal Entry in the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reflect the correct value of the Fixed Asset accounts by capitalizing the interest on the borrowed funds on the Pamilihang Bayan with Water Treatment Facility project. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The Office of the City Accountant to submit to the Auditor the supporting documents of all accumulated accounts payable as reflected in the  Schedule  of  Accounts  Payable  as  of  December 31, 2018   to  back  up  the  authenticity of the recorded obligations in the books of accounts of the City Government;
Undertake in-depth evaluation to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the recorded payables both for the current and prior years ledgers/files to determine which are valid claims or fictitious accounts; and
The Office of the City Accountant to take up the appropriate reversion/adjustments in the books of the LGU for those established transactions with no valid claimants and with dormant accounts in order to have a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

Management Comment:
The City Accountant agreed to trace out in their records the supporting documents pertaining to the foregoing accounts and will make the necessary adjustments for those identified accounts which payments has already been undertaken and those accounts with unavailable supporting documents on file.

We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer promptly prepare the disbursement vouchers covering the remittances and supporting documents pertaining to the collections and prepare the checks covering the said remittances and transmit to the concerned agencies on or before the due date to avoid surcharges and penalties.

Management Comment:
According to the City Accountant, partial remittances were already made on January 2019.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant and the Acting City Treasurer to immediately release the respective amount payable to the 30 barangays of the City Government as well as to the Province of Masbate, as mandated under the existing regulations.
Further, the Office of the City Accountant exert extra efforts to identify the specific barangays for the beginning balance amounting to ₱2.24 million without schedule/details in order to determine the precise amount due to the concerned barangays. Appropriate status report/analysis thereof be immediately furnished to the Audit Team for reference and verification. Any errors discovered in the evaluation must be accordingly adjusted to arrive at the correct amount due to the respective creditors. After which, prepare the respective remittance vouchers to the particular barangays for immediate release of the corresponding checks as mandated in Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code.
Further, to require the Offices of the City Accountant and City Treasurer to adhere strictly to the guidelines relative to the prompt release of the barangay shares from realty taxes as stated under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code and shares from CTCs collections in the ensuing years.

Management Comment:
The LGU officials were receptive of the audit recommendation and promised to comply in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Office of the City Economic Enterprise provide all the pertinent records on the Masbate City Public Market leaseholders to the Office of the City Accountant for the latter to set up the receivable accounts and record its corresponding subsidiary ledgers in the books of accounts of the City Government. Close coordination of the aforementioned Offices is further recommended to evaluate the appropriate amount of receivables from the leaseholders to be recorded in the books of the LGU in order to reflect the accurate balances of its receivables and income accounts.

Management Comment:
The concerned officials were amenable with the observations.   During our conference, the Acting City Economic Enterprise Manager on Market Operations stated that MOAs/Contracts, financial reports and other documents on Market Leaseholders are available and maintained in their Office.  They promised to furnish these documents to the Office of the City Accountant.

We recommended that Management require both the Accounting and Budget Offices to strictly comply with the requirement in the preparation of SCBAA pursuant to IPSAS 24.  See to it that the budgetary basis and classification basis adopted in the approved budget are included in the Notes to Financial Statements. It is understood that the SCBAA is prepared on a comparable basis, otherwise a separate SCBAA is presented to ensure that: a) readers do not misinterpret financial information that is prepared on different bases; b) the financial statements could usefully clarify that the budget and the accounting bases differ; and c) that the SCBAA is prepared on the budget basis (paragraph 23 of IPSAS 24).

Management Comment:
Both the City Budget Officer and City Accountable agreed to the above observations.  They promised to conform with the Accounting Standard in the preparation of the SCBAA.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Local Finance Committee to conduct thorough evaluation of the budget proposals pertaining to the F/P/A under Lump sum Appropriations; consider the analysis of appropriation and expenditure items in conformity with the Standard Government Chart of Accounts and require the WFP before the issuance of AA in order to determine and monitor the accomplishments as against the targets;
b. Authorize the Sangguniang Panglungsod for the LCE to enter into: 1) contract for projects described in generic terms; and 2) contract for purchase of goods and service are necessary for lump sum appropriation; and
c. Require the submission of MOA for fund assistance to other National Government Agencies, approved Program of Work and approved project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses indicating the project/program objective and expected outputs. For recording purposes of the fund assistance, proper coordination with the recipient agency must be undertaken.

Management Comment:
The Local Officials concerned admitted the errors committed.  Both the City Accountant and City Budget Officer agreed to implement the recommendations in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Office of the City Accountant to install control measures on the observance of the required timelines in the submission of accounts/reports to the Office of the Auditor. Further, require the Office of the City Treasurer to immediately submit the paid vouchers/payrolls accounts and issued ORs to the Office of the City Accountant for recording in the Agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month including their supporting documents pursuant to Section 107 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006.
It was suggested that the Office of the City Treasurer submit the monthly accounts on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month to the Office of the City Accountant to ensure compliance with the required deadlines.
Moreover, we reiterated the compliance on the submission of the Accountant’s Advice for all fund disbursements of the Agency. Prompt submission of accounts and other mandated reports will facilitate timely review and verification of the Agency’s financial transactions, thus, corrective measures can be implemented, if necessary.

Management Comment:
The Local Chief Executive instructed the concerned department heads as well as the specific personnel in-charge to see to it that the audit recommendation will be fully implemented in the ensuing year.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking supporting documents.  Henceforth, direct the City Accountant or the accounting personnel to have a thorough review on the completeness of the supporting documents to be attached to the claim for the payment of procurement of infrastructure projects and impose strictly the proper documentation before processing.

Management Comment:
During our meeting with the City Accountant and City Engineer, the latter explained that the lacking documents, particularly inspection reports and pictures are available in their Office. They promised to comply with the recommendations in the succeeding year.

We   recommended   that   the   Local   Chief   Executive direct  the  concerned personnel of the City Government to immediately formulate and submit the  5-Year Staffing Plan as well as the 5-Year Business Plan (BP) in compliance with the provisions of Sections 5.2 and 5.3 of the Manual on Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises in order to aid Management in optimizing the potential of the Local Economic Enterprise of the LGU towards financial  self-sufficiency and a source of local revenues for economic benefits of the Agency in the ensuing years.

Management Comment:
The Local Officials concerned acknowledged the above observations and promised to abide the suggested recommendations.

observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, the balance of Cash Local Treasury amounted to Ᵽ6,417,011.54 showing a decrease of Ᵽ893,745 as compared to CY 2017 balance of Ᵽ7,310,756.54.  The amount of Ᵽ2,083,883.35 was deposited in January 2019.
observations.reason: Part of the balance pertains to prior year’s accounts which are still subject to further verification by the accounting office due to non-maintenance of subsidiary ledgers.  Reconciliation of account is still on-going including the inventory of all issued accountable forms to collectors.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that the concerned Agency officials must strictly abide to the above-cited guidelines on cash advances. Also, require the City Accountant to stop the practice of granting cash advances to officials and employees who have outstanding balanced. The City Accountant must see to it that all outstanding balance of cash advances are fully liquidated every end of the year and ensure that the corresponding liquidations are recorded to their appropriate accounts in order to present fairly the assets and expenses accounts of the city.
Cause for the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances amounting to Ᵽ4.98 million as of December 31, 2017 and submit the liquidation report to the Auditor for post-audit. Send Demand Letters to the concerned Accountable Officer(s) with unliquidated cash advances. Appropriate legal action against those erring officials/employees must be initiated for continued non-compliance with the existing laws and regulations, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: (See related finding No. 1)
observations.reason: Appropriate action was undertaken by requesting the officials and employees with outstanding accounts to immediately settle their accounts.

observations.managementAction:
Partial Inventory Report as of December 31, 2018 was already prepared.
(See Restated under Audit Observation No. 2)
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee has not yet completed the physical count of all properties since the Office of the GSO is still in the process of tracing out the property records and identifying/classifying the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accounting Unit exhaust all possible means to determine the dates of acquisition of the depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment mentioned above. In addition, compute and record immediately the depreciation in the books of accounts in order to present fairly the value of the Property, Plant and Equipment in the Financial Statements.

observations.managementAction: On-going verification of records and review of depreciation accounting for public infrastructures.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office is in the process of establishing the records of accounts under  Office Buildings and other depreciable assets cited in the AAR and reviewing the propriety of applicability of depreciation accounting for public infrastructures account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books pertaining to the understatement of the set-up of SET Receivables for CY 2016 and 2017. Likewise, instruct the City Assessor and the Officer -in-Charge (OIC), City Treasurer to immediately submit the complete Assessment Roll and RPTAR to the City Accountant in order to facilitate the setting up of RPT ad SET receivables account. Likewise, collectibles for the prior year’s and collections thereto must be kept updated through the maintenance of subsidiary ledger cards to ensure the reliability of the account balance of Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables and the corresponding Liability Accounts under Deferred Income Accounts.

We recommended that |Management require the concerned LGU personnel to immediately prepare and submit the liquidation reports with complete supporting documents relative to the utilization of trust funds received from these National Government Agencies. It must be assured that proper reporting and accounting is made on the utilization of the fund transfers to fairly present financial transactions at year-end. See to it that reconciliation of the records between the LGU as Implementing Agency and the Source Agencies be undertaken and appropriate adjustments/corrections, if any, be made in the books. Require the City Accountant to trace out the specific account for the balance of Ᵽ6,735,882.88 without the supporting schedules.
For the excess funds from completed projects, we recommended that these must be returned to the Source Agency in compliance with the guidelines of COA Circular No. 94-013. Require the officials concerned the immediate implementation of priority projects/programs given by National Government Agencies.
observations.managementAction: Refund of balances was remitted to concerned agencies.
observations.reason: There are still documents that are in process and for utilization.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the CDRRMO to submit an updated City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (CDRRMFIP) as recommended by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) showing the programs, projects and activities that must be incorporated in the local development plan and annual work and financial plan of the city government as well as the inclusion on the use of QRF. The City Budget Officer (CBO) must see to it that in the preparation of the budget proposal for the ensuing year, the approved LRRMFIP be the basis in making the current year appropriations for QRF, MF-MOOE and MF-CO.
In addition, submit the Sangguniang Panlungsod Resolution adopting the declaration of calamity in Masbate City as declared by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan per Resolution No. 167-2017 declaring the Province of Masbate under the state of calamity caused by Tropical Depression “Urduja” and authority to utilize the 30% QRF for CY 2017.
Also,  the  CDRRMC must revisit the allocation/utilization of the calamity fund, particularly the Quick Response Fund, in order to have the allocation pf funds proportionate to the extent of damage of the calamity that nay hit the area of jurisdiction of the city government. Henceforth, strict adherence to COA Circular No. 2012-002 is enjoined.

Considering the longer period of time that has elapsed which is almost six months from the targeted date of its completion on October 7, 2017, it is suggested that Management undertake speedy resolution to cause the immediate completion of the project. Otherwise, enforce appropriate legal action against the contractor for damages, if warranted.

We recommended, that the GAD Focal Point System ensure that:
The DILG, Regional Office issue the Certificate of Endorsement on the submitted LGU GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and Review of GAD Accomplishment Report (GAD AR);
GAD Database be developed and maintained as well as regularly updated as sources of relevant information for a systematic planning, programming and policy formulation in order to address the priority gender issues; and
Gender analysis be conducted on the PPAs implemented to determine which among them are responsive to the identified gender issues; and
  Monitoring and evaluation system be established to         periodically assess the GAD related accomplishments and significant concerns.
observations.managementAction: The agency was able to submit the GAD reports to PPDO & DILG for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to Section 7, 8 and 9 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 governing the rendition of accounts and submission of copies of contracts. Direct the BAC Secretariat, City Engineer and City Planning and Development Coordinator to closely coordinate with one another in the compliance of the required documentation and submission to the Audit Team. Further, it is suggested that the Office of the City Accountant require the attachment of complete supporting documents on the duly processed disbursements vouchers pertaining to infrastructure projects before effecting the payment.
observations.managementAction: (See Restated Audit Observation No. 10)
observations.reason: Due to incomplete documentation they were not able to submit the monthly disbursement accounts within the prescribed period.  Simple inadvertence from the BAC in connection with the preparation of supporting documents was also mentioned as one of the reasons.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management consider the creation of special account for economic enterprise for its market operation and require the Office of the City Accountant, in close coordination with the Economic Enterprise Manager, the preparation of the corresponding financial reports separate from the General Fund-Proper in compliance with Section 313 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 105, Chapter 6, Vol. I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs. This will serve as an effective tool in strategy formulation and decision-making activities of the senior management.
Moreover, pass a resolution through the Sanggunian Panlungsod authorizing the LGU for the opening of a separate bank account for Market Operations of Masbate City.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office has already prepared the Financial Statements for the Market Operations and Slaughterhouse. But the opening of the depository account was not yet maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the previous ICO, City Treasurer and Collectors to immediately deposit/remit the balance of ₱3,665,441.66 in the ensuing year. Also, require all accountable Officers to desist from handling huge amount of cash in their possession and deposit all their collections for personal or other purposes. Further, initiate appropriate disciplinary action against those erring accountable officers for continued and/or deliberate non-compliance with the existing laws and regulations, if warranted.
observations.reason: Part of the balance pertains to prior year’s accounts which are still subject to further verification by the accounting office due to non-maintenance of subsidiary ledgers.  Reconciliation of account is still on-going including the inventory of all issued accountable forms to collectors.

observations.managementAction: Appropriate action was undertaken by requesting the officials and employees with outstanding accounts to immediately settle their accounts.
observations.reason:
Restated finding- Please see related Audit Observation
 No.  1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management (a) convene the Inventory Committee and fast track the conduct of physical count, report preparation, and the reconciliation of the inventory results with the ledgers; (b) require the General Services Officer to maintain records on all properties of the agency; and (c) require the City Accountant to properly maintain the subsidiary records of all PPE accounts indicating the complete information and analyze and determine the composition of beginning balances of some PPE categories without the supporting schedules.
Strict adherence to existing laws, auditing rules and regulations in the proper management of government properties is required.
observations.managementAction: The agency was able to prepare the subsidiary records of some PPE items.
observations.reason: Restated finding- Please see related Audit Observation  No.  2.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Office of the City Accountant to submit to the Auditor the supporting documents together with the schedule of all accumulated accounts payable as of December 31, 2016 to back up the authenticity of the recorded obligations in the books o accounts of the city government. We suggested that in depth evaluation be undertaken to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the recorded payables for the current year, with emphasis on the prior year’s ledgers/files to determine which are  valid or fictitious accounts, Further, for those established transactions with no valid claimants and dormant accounts, the City Accountant must take up the appropriate reversion/adjustments in the books of the LGU for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant was able to present the schedules and some documents for the Accounts Payable recorded as of December 31, 2017.
observations.reason: Evaluation and validations of claims and supporting documents are still on-going. Rectification and adjustments is in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive require the City Accountant to trace out the entries made pertaining to the above-mentioned disbursements and appropriate adjusting entries be undertaken in the books. Strict adherence to the guidelines in the accounting and obligations in the disbursement of local fund is required.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant has partially made adjustments on the prior period errors recorded in the books.
observations.reason: We noted that in the calendar year 2018, the agency still have charges to Prior Period Adjustment account for CY 2017 expenditures due to non recording of accounts payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to submit the schedule/details of the computation for the monthly depreciation expense of the property, plant and equipment in order to check the correct balance of the account as presented in the year-end financial statements. Strict adherence to the accounting policies and procedures relative to the costing of property, plant and equipment is required.
observations.managementAction: Schedules of some depreciable properties were presented to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: We have noted that there are PPEs that the agency was unable to set up corresponding depreciation expenses. Please see related finding-Audit Observation No. 4 RE: non-provision of Depreciation Expenses for infrastructure assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Office of the City Accountant to strictly maintain the required Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs). We would like to emphasize that the SLs must be reconciled to their respective general ledger control account balances. Moreover, we strongly  recommended that the Office of the City Accountant evaluate the existing Subsidiary Ledgers to determine the cause of the abnormal balances thereof. Appropriate adjustment must be undertaken to be backed up by working papers or documents for the errors that may be discovered in the evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The SLs of some accounts were already maintained.
observations.reason: Evaluation of the year end supporting schedules disclosed that there are still existing abnormal accounts and unreconciled beginning balances of SL accounts which does not tally with the general ledger controlling balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to immediately prepare and submit to COA the above-mentioned CRAAF for the calendar years 2009 to CY 2016 and every month thereafter. Likewise, require ICO, City Treasurer to submit the same report for the period from August 2016 to December 2016. In addition, require the CGSO to stop the practice of releasing additional accountable forms to the City Treasurer’s Office and Barangay Treasurers without requiring the submission of the copies of the Report of Collections and Deposits corresponding to the issuance of the previous ORs/Accountable Forms. See to it that proper coordination with the Accounting office is undertaken to ensure that all collections have been recorded in the books of city and barangays. Strict compliance with the provisions of Section 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II, is required.

We recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System must promptly prepare and submit the agency’s GAD Plan and  Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February  of each year to see to it that the GPB of the city is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB must be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. The certificate of approval from the  latter will ensure that the programs/projects/activities reflected in the GPB are in accordance with the provisions of the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001 and other existing regulations/guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The agency was able to submit the GAD reports to PPDO & DILG for review.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Require the City Accountant to prepare a Disbursement Voucher for the transfer of the unutilized balance of ₱13,820,089.81 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM. Ensure that the unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE for each year be timely transferred at year-end with the corresponding cash back-up;
The Accounting Office to coordinate with the Budget Office to reconcile their records and obtain the correct amount of the unexpended balances; and
Require the City Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger of the Trust Liability-DRRMF account by source and by year when the savings were realize.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Office of the City Treasurer to immediately submit the paid vouchers/payrolls accounts and issued ORs to the Office of the  City Accountant for recording in the agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing including their supporting  documents pursuant to Section 107 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006.

It is suggested that the Office of the City Treasurer submit the monthly accounts on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month to the Office of the City Accountant to ensure compliance with the required deadlines. Prompt submission of accounts and other mandated reports will facilitate timely review and verification of the agency’s financial transactions, thus corrective measures can be implemented, if necessary.

The Management and the concerned officials and employees are also reminded of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445, Section 23 (1) (3) of the Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, which may be imposed as a consequence of their failure or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant had exerted efforts to partially comply with the submission timelines.  Additional manpower is now being task to assist.
observations.reason: Still not within the required timeline due to the voluminous documents and attachments to be made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Office of the City Treasurer to immediately submit the Disbursement Vouchers including their supporting documents for checks omitted from the Reports of Checks Issued (RCIs) to the Office of the City Accountant for recording in the agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the soonest possible time in compliance with Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445. Prompt submission of accounts and other mandated reports will facilitate timely review and verification of the agency’s financial transactions and appropriate audit action or corrective measures, if necessary, will be undertaken to prevent adverse audit actions. Moreover, it will enhance fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements since all actual disbursements will be accordingly recorded in the books for the period to which they have occurred.

We suggested that the Management evaluate their existing internal control system in the actual procedure of handling the check disbursement transactions particularly on the custodial functions/release of the disbursement vouchers with their complete supporting documents at the Office of the City Treasurer to ensure that all accounts/ documents are forwarded  to the Office of the City Accountant on the required deadline to prevent the further occurrence of this deficiency in the succeeding years.
The Management and the concerned officials/employees are also reminded of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, which may be imposed as a consequence of their inability or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause.
observations.managementAction: Policies and measures are now being considered that will strengthen the internal control system of the LGU.
observations.reason: Remaining DVs are still under verification and validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned accountable officers to prepare and update their cashbooks and submit the same to the audit team for verification purposes. It is also reminded that cashbooks must be submitted to the accounting office for reconciliation purposes in order to arrive at the correct and accurate balances of cash accounts as of a specified period. In this regard, the ICO-City Treasurer monitor strict compliance by all accountable officers with the above-cited guidelines in the maintenance of cashbooks.
observations.managementAction: Some Accountable Officers (AOs) have complied the audit recommenda-tion.
observations.reason: Other AOs still unable to properly maintain cashbooks. Reconciliation with the cashbooks and accounting records of prior year’s accounts are still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Office of the City Accountant to immediately submit all Accountant’s Advice of Local Check Disbursements of the city government. See to it that copies of Accountant’s Advice in the ensuing period are regularly and completely submitted to the Audit Team as mandated in COA Circular No. 96-007 dated May 15, 1996. We suggested that it must be forwarded to the Auditor on a weekly basis for all funds per bank account check issuances.
observations.reason: The submitted Accountant’s Advice were returned by the Auditor because of incomplete data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the ICO-City Treasurer and Liquidating Officer to ensure that all collections and issued accountable forms are closely reviewed, scrutinized and where necessary, recalculated in order to avoid incurrence of under collections/under remittances. Require the Collecting Clerks to remit the amount of audit charge. Strict adherence to the above-mentioned guidelines on collections and deposits is required.
observations.managementAction: The new OIC, City Treasurer has introduced measures to strengthen somehow the internal control in the treasury department operations.
observations.reason: The Audit Team observed some lapses in the reporting activities of some collectors as well as the liquidating officer in the treasury department.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to Sections 7, 8 and 9 of COA Circular No. 97-001 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 governing the rendition of accounts and submission of copies of contracts is required. Direct the BAC Secretariat, City Engineer and City Planning and Development Coordinator to closely coordinate in the compliance of the required documentation and submission to the Audit Team. Further, it is suggested that the Office of the City Accountant require the attachment of complete supporting documents on the duly processed disbursement vouchers pertaining to infrastructure projects before effecting the payment.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the concerned officials to submit the required lacking documents. The BAC Secretariat Office partially complied the documentations pertaining to  some project implemented by the city government in CY 2017.
observations.reason: The Audit Team noted some deficiencies relative to the  implementation of various PPAs of the LGU. Lack of supporting documents was still the problem in complying with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to record receivable account from the League to ensure fair presentation of the account.
Henceforth, for control purposes, ensure that the transfer is properly taken up in the books of both agencies, the transferred funds are used only for the intended purpose, and that proper accounting and reporting is made of the utilization of the funds.
observations.managementAction: The city Accountant made necessary adjustments in compliance to COA recommenda-tions.
observations.reason: Securing of Official Receipts for the annual dues is underway.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Office of the City Treasurer conduct intensification of its revenue collection campaign and install appropriate monitoring mechanisms to ensure efficient and effective collection of targeted revenues.

We suggested that the Offices of the City Assessor and City Treasurer coordinate and install measures on how to strengthen the monitoring mechanisms relative to taxes due on real properties and the delinquent taxpayers. Moreover, strengthen the internal control system in the reporting and recording process of collections to eliminate errors and increase actual collections of revenues due for the city government.
observations.managementAction:
The city government initiated an intensified tax campaign making the component barangays partners in the implementation of the program.

Collaborative effort between the City Treasurer’s Office and City Assessor’s Office is working incessantly.
observations.reason: The campaign is still on-going

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management: (a.) Cause for the immediate deposit of collections amounting to ₱1,923,859.54, otherwise the same will be considered as shortage of the accountable officers concerned. (b.) Direct the City Treasurer to deposit intact all his collections and compel all accountable officers/collectors to remit all their collections for the day regardless of the amount. (c.) Appropriate legal action should be initiated against accountable officers/collectors who continuously defy existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance or deposit of collections.
observations.managementAction:
The Honorable Mayor called the attention of all concerned accountable officers to settle immediately their accounts.
Partial remittances of prior year’s balance were made during the year.
observations.reason: Prior Year’s accounts are still subject to further verification particularly those accounts without subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the management of LGU-Masbate City: (a.) Strictly enforce settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2015, particularly those of the previous years. (b.) Stop the granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled/liquidated to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances. (c.) Send demand letters to all concerned requiring the immediate settlement and/or refund of the amount advanced, and enforce the withholding of salaries of those who fail to settle/liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period after the demand. (d.) Initiate appropriate legal/disciplinary action against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws and regulations, if warranted.
observations.managementAction:
Demand letters were issued to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances.  Liquidation of some employees were settled, thus reducing the balance of outstanding Cash Advances. (Please see related Audit Observation 
No.  1).
observations.reason: Partial remittances were noted in compliance with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the City Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements within ten days from receipt of the monthly bank statements for the Auditor to have ample time in the verification of the report.  See to it that the Cash in Bank balances in the books are already adjusted and reconciled with the records/cashbooks of the City Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant had furnished the Audit Team Bank Reconcilia-tion Statements for the General Fund only. But, the accompanying documents were not  submitted together with the report. In this regard, the LCE required the Accounting Office to take action on this finding.
observations.reason: No adjustments has been made for the reconciling items per Bank Reconciliation Statement as of November 30, 2016, including the missing disbursement vouchers omitted in the RCIs but debited per bank statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency strictly comply with the proper procedure and accounting treatment in the recording of Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables as prescribed under Sections 246 & 248 of R.A. 7160 and Section 20 of the New Government Accounting System manual for Local Government Units, Volume 1.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office was still unable to maintain subsidiary ledgers for individual taxpayers hence, t RPTAR and SET Receivables on prior year’s accounts could not be ascertained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant record in the books of accounts the cost of the equipment and make the necessary adjusting entries in order to present the true financial position of the City Government. The Management and the concerned City Officials are also reminded of the administrative or criminal liability which may be imposed as a consequence of its failure to account without justifiable cause.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management direct the City General Service Officer (CGSO), City Accountant and Inventory Committee to finish the physical inventory of the City’s properties and to furnish the Auditor with a copy of the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment  to facilitate the verification and audit of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.  Properties found to be unserviceable should be disposed of immediately.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE required the GSO and other concerned officials to conduct the inventory of all properties of the city.
(Please see related Audit Observation 
No.  2).
observations.reason:
Partial report was submitted by the CGSO but considered as a mere listing only of the assets owned by the city government based on the office records.
The City General Services Officer (CGSO) and Inventory Committee was not able complete the conduct of the required inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant fast track the review of all outstanding accounts payable,  and revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund those found not supported with valid/actual claims and obligations that remained unliquidated for years,  to avoid a yearly overstatement of  accounts payable.
observations.managementAction: Per instruction of the LCE, the accounting office was able to trace out some of the accounts in the schedules for prior year’s.
observations.reason: The Office of the City Accountant is still unable to fully evaluate the individual accounts that comprise the Accounts Payable. We noted that prior year’s accounts still remained in the books as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management remit in full and within the prescribed period all taxes withheld from individuals and suppliers in accordance with Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 and Republic Act No. 8424.
observations.managementAction: Some of the Prior year’s accounts were already remitted.
observations.reason: There are prior accounts that still remained unremitted due to absence of schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to submit immediately all accounts and financial reports under each fund for the Audit Team to review on time the transactions and operations of the City Government.  Also, Management should consider timely submission of reports as among the primary measures of good governance and performance. Moreover, we recommended that proper division/distribution of accounting workload be made and proper delegation to other employees be done.  Management should also send staff to appropriate trainings to properly equipped them and to have a complete knowledge of the entire accounting system/process.
observations.managementAction: The Honorable Mayor gave instructions to the Accounting and Treasury Offices to immediately look into their records and solve the problem.
observations.reason: Some Disbursement Vouchers lacked the complete supporting documents which contributed to the delay in the submission of the Accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chief Executive require the City Engineer and the City Planning Development Coordinator to provide the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year.  Also, require them to prepare and submit to the Audit Team every end of the quarter the report  on publicized PPAs for monitoring as well as auditing purposes of the status of implementation of each project.
observations.managementAction: Some of the on-going PPAs were instead reported in the Quarterly Report of Publicized PPAs.
observations.reason: Not all on going projects are reflected in the report.  Separate report/list should have been prepared for at the onset of the calendar year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Office of the City Treasurer to immediately submit the Disbursement Vouchers including their supporting documents for checks omitted from the Reports of Checks Issued (RCIs) to the Office of the City Accountant for recording in the agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the soonest possible time in compliance with Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445. Prompt submission of accounts and other mandated reports will facilitate timely review and verification of the agency’s financial transactions and appropriate audit action or corrective measures, if necessary, will be undertaken to prevent adverse audit actions. Moreover, it will enhance fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements since all actual disbursements will be accordingly recorded in the books for the period to which they have occurred.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented. As of this date, the agency still not able to submit the aforementioned disbursements accounts.
observations.reason: The agency still not able to submit the aforementioned disbursements accounts.
