We recommended that Management require the previous ICO, City Treasurer and Collectors to immediately deposit/remit the balance of P3,665,441.66 in the ensuing year. Also, require all accountable Officers to desist from handling huge amount of cash in their possession and deposit all their collections for personal or other purposes. Further, initiate appropriate disciplinary action against those erring accountable officers for continued and/or deliberate non-compliance with the existing laws and regulations, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented. Collections totaling $7,310,756.54 as of December 31, 2017 remained undeposited was partially deposited in CY 2017
observations.reason: The ~ balance of $7,310,756.54 as of December 31, 2017 remained undeposited.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Strictly enforce the settlement of the unliquidated cash advances of all accountable officers and officials/employees; b. Stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; c. See to it that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year to reflect the correct balances of the accounts affected in the financial statements; d. Direct the Office of the City Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team all liquidation reports and the corresponding supporting documents on the recorded settlement of cash advances to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit; e. Require the City Accountant to monitor the settlement of cash advances; and f. Institute the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Restated finding- Please see related Audit Observation No. 2. The agency has decreased the cash advances balance from P6.04 million for CY 2016 to P4,983,665.43 as of Dec. 31, 2017.
observations.reason: The balance is an accumulation of prior years’ and current year’s balances amounting to 3,121,562.08 and P1,862,103.35, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) convene the Inventory Committee and fast track the conduct of physical count, report preparation, and the reconciliation of the inventory results with the ledgers; (b) require the General Services Officer to maintain records on all properties of the agency; and (c) require the City Accountant to properly maintain the subsidiary records of all PPE accounts indicating the complete information and analyze and determine the composition of beginning balances of some PPE categories without the supporting schedules.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Restated finding- Please see related Audit Observation No. 3. The agency was able to prepare the subsidiary records of some PPE items. Strict adherence to existing laws, auditing rules and regulations in proper management of government properties is required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Office of the City Accountant to submit to the Auditor the supporting documents together with the schedule of all accumulated accounts payable as of December 31, 2016 to back up the authenticity of the recorded obligations in the books of the city government. We suggested that in depth evaluation be undertaken to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the recorded payables for the current year, with emphasis on the prior year’s ledgers/files to determine which are valid or fictitious accounts. For those established transactions with no valid claimants and dormant accounts, the City Accountant must take up the appropriate reversion/adjustments in the books of the LGU for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: However, the corresponding supporting documents/vouchers presented as of December 31, 2016 were presented in the books. The Office of the City Accountant was able to present the schedules and some supporting documents as of December 31, 2017. Review of accounting records showed that no evaluation has been undertaken to establish the validity of claims on the recorded accounts payable. Hence, the account was not appropriately sanitized to present fairly in the financial statements.
observations.reason: No evaluation has been undertaken to establish the validity of claims on the recorded accounts payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended requiring the ICO, City Treasurer to immediately remit or deposit the withheld collections due to BIR, GSIS, PhilHEALTH and Pag-IBIG. Require the City Accountant and ICO, City Treasurer to prepare the schedule of monthly remittances and disbursement vouchers and immediately prepare and release the checks to them. Prompt remittances of these trust liabilities must be done to avoid possible misuse of cash resources and incurrence of unnecessary penalties that may be imposed by the concerned agencies.
observations.managementAction: Per schedule of the year-end financial statements, the total unremitted Trust Receipts to BIR, GSIS, Philhealth and Pag-IBIG amounting P16,200,356.81 as of December 31, 2017 were partially remitted in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to trace out the entries made pertaining to the above-mentioned disbursements and appropriate adjusting entries be undertaken in the books. Strict adherence to the guidelines in the accounting and obligations in the disbursement of local fund is required.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant has partially made adjustments on the calendar year 2017 charges to Prior Period & Adjustments. The Office of the City Accountant has partially made adjustments to the recorded accounts.
observations.reason: The agency still have charges to Prior Period & Adjustments recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to submit the schedule/details of the computation for the monthly depreciation expense of the property, plant and equipment in order to check the correct balance of the account as presented in the year-end financial statements. Strict adherence to the accounting policies and procedures relative to the costing of depreciation expenses.
observations.managementAction: We have noted that there are PPEs that the agency was unable to set-up corresponding property, plant and equipment is required.
observations.reason: Schedules of some depreciable properties were presented to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Office of the City Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs). We would like to emphasize that the SLs must be reconciled to their respective general ledger control account balances. Moreover, we strongly recommended that the Office of the City Accountant evaluate the existing Subsidiary Ledgers to determine the cause of the abnormal balances thereof. Appropriate adjustment must be undertaken to be backed up by working papers or documents for the errors that may be discovered in the evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Evaluation of the year end supporting schedules disclosed that there are still existing abnormal accounts and unreconciled beginning balances of SL accounts which does not tally with the general ledger controlling balances. The SLs of some accounts were already maintained.
observations.reason: There are still existing abnormal accounts and unreconciled beginning balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to immediately prepare and submit to COA the above-mentioned CRAAF for the calendar years 2009 to CY 2016 and every month thereafter. Likewise, require ICO, City Treasurer to submit the same report for the period from August 2016 to December 2016. In addition, require the CGSO to stop the practice of releasing additional accountable forms to the City Treasurer’s Office and Barangay Treasurers without requiring the submission of the copies of the Report of Collections and Deposits corresponding to the issuance of the previous-ORs/Accountable Forms. See to it that proper coordination with the Accounting office is undertaken to ensure that all collections have been recorded in the books of city and barangays. Strict compliance with the provisions of Section 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II, is required.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented. SL accounts were already maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System must promptly prepare and submit the agency’s GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of 'January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the city is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB must be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. The certificate of approval from the latter will ensure that the programs/projects/activities reflected in the GPB are in accordance with the provisions of the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001 and other existing regulations/guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The city government was not able to secure a Certificate of Approval from the DILG for review. The agency was_able to submit the GAD reports to PPDO & DILG, Reg. Office to ensure that the GPB are in accordance with the provisions of JMC No. 2013-001.
observations.reason: No Certificate of Endorsement has been issued by DILG, Regional Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. Require the City Accountant to prepare a Disbursement Voucher for the transfer of the unutilized balance of P13,820,089.81 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM. Ensure that the unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE for each year be timely transferred at year-end with the corresponding cash back-up; b. The Accounting Office to coordinate with the Budget Office to reconcile their records and obtain the correct amount of the unexpended balances; and c. Require the City Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger of the Trust Liability-DRRMF account by source and by year when the savings were realize.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the Office of the City Treasurer to immediately submit the paid vouchers/payrolls accounts and issued ORs to the Office of the City Accountant for recording in the agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing including their supporting documents pursuant to Section 107 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006. It is suggested that the Office of the City Treasurer submit the monthly accounts on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month to the Office of the City Accountant to ensure compliance with the required deadlines. Prompt submission of accounts and other mandated reports will facilitate timely review and verification of the agency’s financial transactions, thus corrective measures can be implemented, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The agency was not able to submit the CY 2017 monthly accounts/reports within the required deadlines. The CY 2017 year-end FS was submitted on March 28, 2018, forty two (42) days beyond the legal deadline. The Office of the City Accountant had exerted efforts to partially comply with the submission timelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned accountable officers to prepare and update their cashbooks and submit the same to the audit team for verification purposes. It is also reminded that cashbooks must be submitted to the accounting office for reconciliation purposes in order to arrive at the correct and accurate balances of cash accounts as of a specified period. In this regard, the ICO-City Treasurer monitor strict compliance by all accountable officers with the above-cited guidelines in the maintenance of cashbooks.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Other AOs still unable to properly maintain cashbooks. With related finding per Audit Observation No. 1. Some Accountable Officers (AOs) have complied the audit re-commendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Office of the City Accountant to immediately submit all Accountant’s Advice of Local Check Disbursements of the city government. See to it that copies of Accountant’s Advice in the ensuing period are regularly and completely submitted to the Audit Team as mandated in COA Circular No. 96-007 dated May 15, 1996. We suggested that it must be forwarded to the Auditor on a weekly basis for all funds per bank account check issuances.

We recommended that Management require the ICO-City Treasurer and Liquidating Officer to ensure that all collections and issued accountable forms are closely reviewed, scrutinized and where necessary, recalculated in order to avoid incurrence of under collections/under remittances. Require the Collecting Clerks to remit the amount of audit charge. Strict adherence to the above-mentioned guidelines on collections and deposits is required.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to Sections 7, 8 and 9 of COA Circular No. 97-001 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 governing the rendition of accounts and submission of copies of contracts is required. Direct the BAC Secretariat, City Engineer and City Planning and Development Coordinator to closely coordinate in the compliance of the required documentation and submission to the Audit Team. Further, it is suggested that the Office of the City Accountant require the attachment of complete supporting documents on the duly processed disbursement vouchers pertaining to infrastructure projects before effecting the payment.
observations.managementAction: Noted some lapses in the reporting activities of some collectors as well as the liquidating officer in the treasury department. The new OIC, City Treasurer has introduced measures to strengthen somehow the internal control in the treasury department operations. The LCE instructed the concerned officials to submit the required lacking documents. The BAC Secretariat Office partially complied the documentation.
observations.reason: Some documents were not submitted within required timeframe; incomplete supporting documents caused delay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to record receivable account from the League to ensure fair presentation of the account. Henceforth, for control purposes, ensure that the transfer is properly taken up in the books of both agencies, the transferred funds are used only for the intended purpose, and that proper accounting and reporting is made of the utilization of the funds.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The City Accountant was unable to record the receivable account from the League to ensure that the transferred amount is properly taken up in the books of the LGU. Some related documentations relative to League transactions were furnished by the City Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Office of the City Treasurer conduct intensification of its revenue collection campaign and install appropriate monitoring mechanisms to ensure efficient and collection of targeted revenues. We suggested that the Offices of the City Assessor and City Treasurer coordinate and install measures on how to strengthen the monitoring mechanisms relative to taxes due on real properties and the delinquent taxpayers. Moreover, strengthen the internal control system in the reporting and recording process of collections to eliminate errors and increase actual collections of revenues due for the city government.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The city government was able to introduce innovations in the treasury department to enhance its collection efficiency. Noted that the collection for realty taxes has increased in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management: (a.) Cause for the immediate deposit of collections amounting to £1,923,859.54, otherwise the same will be considered as shortage of the accountable officers concerned. (b.) Direct the City Treasurer to deposit intact all his collections and compel all accountable officers/collectors to remit all their collections for the day regardless of the amount. (c.) Appropriate legal action should be initiated against accountable officers/collectors who continuously defy existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance or deposit of collections.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Reminded collectors of the prompt remittance of their collections. Demand letters were issued to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances. Partial remittances of prior year’s balance were made during the year.
observations.reason: Not all on going projects are reflected in the report. Some prior year’s accounts remained undeposited.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation for the City Treasurer of Masbate to deposit in full the undeposited collections as of December 31, 2014 of $2,183,497.57, and for the City Treasurer and Cashier to deposit intact and in full all their collections. Likewise, all Accountable Officers/Collectors should be required to remit all their collections for the day. Appropriate legal/disciplinary action should be initiated against accountable officers/collectors who continuously defy existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of collections.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The Honorable Mayor called the attention of all concerned accountable officers to settle immediately their accounts. Demand letters were issued to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances. Liquidation of some employees were settled, thus reducing the balance of outstanding Cash Advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the City Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements within ten days from receipt of the monthly bank statements for the Auditor to have ample time in the verification of the report. See to it that the Cash in Bank balances in the books are already adjusted and reconciled with the per bank statements and the records/cashbooks of the City Treasurer. In this regard, the LCE required the Accounting Office to take action on this finding.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The Office of the City Accountant furnished the Audit Team Bank Reconciliation Statement as of the reconciliation thereon. But, the disbursement vouchers omitted in the RCIs but debited per bank statements, the accompanying documents were not submitted together with the report. In this regard, the LCE required the Accounting Office to take action on this finding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency strictly comply with the proper procedure and accounting treatment in the recording of Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables as prescribed under Sections 246 & 248 of R.A. 7160 and Section 20 of the New Government Accounting System manual for Local Government Units, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The set-up of SET Receivables was based on the estimated income. Evaluation of CY 2016 General Ledger receivable accounts showed that the agency had set up on Jan. 1, 2016 the realty receivable accounts.
observations.reason: The set-up of SET Receivables was based on the estimated income and remained understated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant record in the books of accounts the cost of the equipment and make the necessary adjusting entries in order to present the true financial position of the City Government. The Management and the concerned City Officials are also reminded of the administrative or criminal liability which may be imposed as a consequence of its failure to account without justifiable cause.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management direct the City General Service Officer (CGSO), City Accountant and Inventory Committee to finish the physical inventory of the City’s properties and to furnish the Auditor with a copy of the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to facilitate the verification and audit of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts. Properties found to be unserviceable should be disposed of immediately.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Partial report was submitted by the CGSO but considered as a mere listing only. The LCE required the GSO and other concerned officials to conduct the inventory of all properties of the city.
observations.reason: The CGSO and Inventory Committee was not able complete the conduct of the required inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant fast track the review of all outstanding accounts payable, and revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund those found not supported with valid/actual claims and obligations that remained unliquidated for years; to avoid a yearly overstatement of accounts-payable. We recommended that in depth evaluation be undertaken to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the recorded payables for the current year, with emphasis on the prior year’s ledgers/files to determine which are valid or fictitious accounts.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Per-instruction-of-the LCE, the accounting office was able to trace out some of the prior year’s accounts. The Office of the City Accountant is still unable to fully evaluate the individual items that comprise the accounts payable for the prior years. The accounting office was able to trace out some of the items.
observations.reason: Some prior year's accounts remained in the books as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management remit in full and within the prescribed period all taxes withheld from individuals and suppliers in accordance with Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 and Republic Act No. 8424.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. There are prior accounts that still remained unremitted. Some of the Prior year’s accounts were already remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to submit immediately all accounts and financial reports under each fund for the Audit Team to review on time the transactions and operations of the City Government. Also, Management should consider timely submission of reports as among the primary measures of good governance and performance. Moreover, we recommended that proper division/distribution of accounting workload be made and proper delegation to other employees be done. Management should also send staff to appropriate trainings to properly equipped them and to have a complete knowledge of the entire accounting system/process.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Some Disbursement Vouchers are missing which contributed to the delay in the submission of the financial reports. The Honorable Mayor gave instructions to the Accounting and Treasury Offices to immediately look into the disbursements vouchers missing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chief Executive require the City provide the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year. Also, require them to prepare and submit to the Audit Team every end of the quarter the report on publicized PPAs for monitoring as well as auditing purposes of the status of implementation of each project.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Not all on going projects are reflected in the report. Separate report/list should have been prepared for at the onset of the calendar year.
observations.reason: Some of the on-going PPAs were instead reported in the Quarterly Report of Publicized PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation for the City Treasurer of Masbate to deposit in full the undeposited collections as of December 31, 2014 of $2,183,497.57, and for the City Treasurer and Cashier to deposit intact and in full all their collections. Likewise, all Accountable Officers/Collectors should be required to remit all their collections for the day. Appropriate legal/disciplinary action should be initiated against accountable officers/collectors who continuously defy existing laws, rules and regulations on deposit of collections.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Reminded collectors of the prompt remittance of their collections. Some of the on-going projects are reflected in the report. The former OIC City Treasurer was not yet completed. The LCE required the Accounting Office to strictly implement the COA rules and regulations in the practice of the City Treasurer.
observations.reason: There are still prior year’s accounts that remained undeposited.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation for the City Treasurer to deposit in full the undeposited collections and to enforce settlement of unliquidated cash advances. Stop granting additional cash advances unless previous ones are settled, send demand letters, enforce withholding of salaries of those who fail to settle, and initiate appropriate legal/disciplinary action if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Demand letters were issued to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances. Liquidation of some employees were settled, thus reducing the balance of outstanding Cash Advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the City Accountant to prepare or cause the preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements within ten (10) days from receipt of the monthly bank statements and to submit to the Auditor for verification and audit.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Directed the City Accountant to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The account balance was still not adjusted in the books nor reconciled the treasury records.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management direct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory of the City’s properties and to furnish the Auditor with a copy of the Inventory Report upon completion to facilitate the verification and audit of the Property, Plant and Equipment account.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. No RPCPPE was submitted to the Audit Team for CY 2017. Combined and coordinated effort is being required between the accounting and City General Service Offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant look into the details and exert extra efforts to the reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect immediately the necessary adjustments in the books in order to present a more reliable financial statements in the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Still in the process of tracing out in the records the details of the accounts. Directed the City Accountant to correct/adjust the discrepancies noted in the reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the City Accountant to prepare a DV for the transfer of the unutilized balance of the LDRRM Fund amounting to P24,666,575.54 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund under the account, Trust Liability-DRRM (Code 438).
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management require the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to promptly submit monthly reports, trial balances and other financial reports to apprise the Chief Executive of the status of the finances and accounts of the City, which is needed for a sound decision making, and for the Auditor to review on time the accounts and operations of the City.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Very much delayed submission of the financial reports and statements was still noted. Concerted and coordinated effort is being required between the accounting and treasury officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require all accountable officers/collectors in the City Government to maintain an updated cashbooks in accordance with the provisions of the NGAS Manual and ensure that recording of all transactions involving money handled/received by them are proper and on a daily basis. Also, regular checking/monitoring of cashbooks maintenance helps detect shortage, losses and identify error in recording.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Required the former OIC, City Treasurer to strictly implement the COA rules and regulations in the practice of the custody and recording. The former OIC City Treasurer was not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management direct the City Planning and Development Officer and other officials concerned to ensure that the 20% EDF is fully utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects for the benefit of the constituents of Masbate, by regularly preparing progress/periodic-reports-to-validate-actual-accomplishments-of-the-projects, fast tracking the completion of the remaining on-going and unimplemented projects and by closely coordinating with City officials concerned.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the concerned City Officials to prepare a well-studied project under the 20% Development Fund that can be implemented immediately and completed on time as planned. Also, strict observance of the timetable of the implementation of specific phase of activities to be performed will help the city to maximize the utilization of available funds to attain the desired results.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to submit immediately all accounts and financial reports under each fund for the Audit Team to review on time the transactions and operations of the City Government. Also, Management should consider timely submission of reports as among the primary measures of good governance and performance. Moreover, we recommended that proper division/distribution of accounting workload be made and proper delegation to other employees be done. Management should also send staff to appropriate trainings to properly equipped them and to_have a complete knowledge of the entire accounting system/process.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant is still unable to evaluate the individual accounts that comprise the Accounts Payable. (Please see related finding per Audit Observation Nos. 12 and 13).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the City Accountant and other accountable officers to ensure timely submission of disbursement and collection accounts and supporting documents to the Auditor within the prescribed period to facilitate timely audit verification and review.
observations.managementAction: Some Disbursement Vouchers are missing which contributed to the delay in the submission of accounting reports and the timely review of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We therefore encourage Management to maintain the prestige of being a recipient of the SGLG award. Likewise, it is suggested that Management implement and/or comply with the audit recommendations in order to qualify for the said SGLG taking into consideration the area on the financial administration.

We recommended that Management require the previous ICO, City Treasurer and Collectors to immediately deposit/remit the balance of P3,665,441.66 in the ensuing year. Also, require all accountable Officers to desist from handling huge amount of cash in their possession and deposit all their collections in accordance with the NGAS, Volume I, thus, discouraging them from using their collections for personal or other purposes.  Further, initiate appropriate disciplinary action against those erring accountable officers for continued and/or deliberate non-compliance with the existing laws and regulations, if warranted.

We recommended that Management:

a. Strictly enforce the settlement of the unliquidated cash advances of all
accountable officers and officials/’employees;

b. Stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers,
officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid
accumulation of the same;

c. See to it that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year to
reflect the correct balances of the accounts affected in the financial
statements;

d. Direct the Office of the City Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit
Team all liquidation reports and the corresponding supporting documents on
the recorded settlement of cash advances to avoid suspension or disallowance
in audit;

e. Require the City Accountant to monitor the settlement of cash advances;
and

f. Institute the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring
accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the
existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.

We recommended that management (a) convene the Inventory Committee and fast track the conduct of physical count, report preparation, and the reconciliation of the inventory results with the ledgers; (b) require the General Services Officer to maintain records on all properties of the agency; and (c) require the City Accountant to properly maintain the subsidiary records of all PPE accounts indicating the complete information and analyze and determine the composition of beginning balances of some PPE categories without the supporting schedules.

Strict adherence to existing laws, auditing rules and regulations in the proper
management of government properties is required.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Office of the City Accountant to submit to the Auditor the supporting documents together with the schedule of all accumulated accounts payable as of December 31, 2016 to back up the authenticity of the recorded obligations in the books of accounts of the city government. 'We suggested that in depth evaluation be undertaken to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the recorded payables for the current year, with emphasis on the prior year’s ledgers/files to determine which are valid claims or fictitious accounts. Further, for those established transactions with no valid claimants and dormant accounts, the City Accountant must take up the appropriate reversion/adjustments in the books of the LGU for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended requiring the ICO, City Treasurer to immediately remit or deposit the withheld collections due to BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG. Require the City Accountant and ICO, City Treasurer to prepare the schedule of monthly remittances and disbursement vouchers and immediately prepare and release the checks to them. Prompt remittances of these trust liabilities must be done to avoid possible misuse of cash resources and incurrence of unnecessary penalties that may be imposed by the concerned agencies. Further, it is reminded that payments covering the said penalties and surcharges on the late remittances must not be charged to the government funds but instead a personal liability of the concerned officials who failed to comply with the mandated laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to trace out the entries made pertaining to the above-mentioned disbursements and appropriate adjusting entries be undertaken in the books. Strict adherence to the guidelines in the accounting and obligations in the disbursement of local fund is required.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to submit the schedule/details of the computation for the monthly depreciation expense of the property, plant and equipment in order to check the correct balance of the account as presented in the year-end financial statements. Strict adherence to the accounting policies and procedures relative to the costing of property, plant and equipment is required.

We recommended that Management require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to immediately prepare and submit to COA the above-mentioned CRAAF for the calendar years 2009 to CY 2016 and every month thereafter. Likewise, require ICO, City Treasurer to submit the same report for the period from August 2016 to December 2016. In addition, require CGSO to stop the practice of releasing additional accountable forms to the City Treasurer’s Office and Barangay Treasurers without requiring the submission of the copies of the Report of Collections and Deposits corresponding to the issuance of the previous ORs/Accountable Forms. See to it that proper coordination with the Accounting office is undertaken to ensure that all collections have been recorded in the books of city and barangays. Strict compliance with the provisions of Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II, is required.

We recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point System must promptly prepare and submit the agency’s GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office(PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the city is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB must be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. The certificate of approval from the latter will ensure that the programs/projects/activities reflected in the GPB are in accordance with the provisions of the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001 and other existing regulations/guidelines.

Furthermore, we suggested that the Tourism, Culture and Arts Promotion program/activity would be more appropriate to be included in the regular PPAs under the General Fund instead of including it among the Gender and Development PPAs.

Moreover, the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report must also be submitted to the DILG for the corresponding assessment of the reported PPAs undertaken and for its consolidation to be reported to the Office of the PCW.

We recommended the following:

a. Require the City Accountant to prepare a Disbursement Voucher for the
transfer of the unutilized balance of P13,820,089.81 from the General Fund
to the Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM. Ensure that the
unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE for each year be timely
transferred at year-end with the corresponding cash back up;

b. The Accounting Office to coordinate with the Budget Office to reconcile their
records and obtain the correct amount of the unexpended balances; and

¢. Require the City Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger of the Trust
Liability-DRRMF account by source and by year when the savings were
realize.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Office of the City Treasurer to immediately submit the paid vouchers/payrolls accounts and issued ORs to the Office of the City Accountant for recording in the agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month including their supporting documents pursuant to Section 107 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006.

It is suggested that the Office of the City Treasurer submit the monthly accounts on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month to the Office of the City Accountant to ensure compliance with the required deadlines.

Prompt submission of accounts and other mandated reports will facilitate timely review and verification of the agency’s financial transactions, thus corrective measures can be implemented, if necessary.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Office of the City Treasurer to immediately submit the Disbursement Vouchers including their supporting documents for checks omitted from the Reports of Checks Issued (RCIs) to the Office of the City Accountant for recording in the agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the soonest possible time in compliance with Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445,

Prompt submission of accounts and other mandated reports will facilitate timely review and verification of the agency’s financial transactions and appropriate audit action or corrective measures, if necessary, will be undertaken to prevent adverse audit actions. Moreover, it will enhance fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements since all actual disbursements will be accordingly recorded in the books for the period to which they have occurred.

We suggested that the Management evaluate their existing internal control system in the actual procedure of handling the check disbursement transactions particularly on the custodial functions/release of the disbursement vouchers with their complete supporting documents at the Office of the City Treasurer to ensure that all accounts/documents are forwarded to the Office of the City Accountant on the required deadline to prevent the further occurrence of this deficiency in the succeeding years.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the comcerned accountable officers to prepare and update their cashbooks and submit the same to the audit team for verification purposes. It is also reminded that cashbooks must be submitted to the accounting office for reconciliation purposes in order to arrive at the correct and accurate balances of cash accounts as of a specified period. In this regard, the ICO-City Treasurer monitor strict compliance by all accountable officers with the above-cited guidelines in the maintenance of cashbooks.

We recommended that the Management require the Office of the City Accountant to immediately submit all Accountant’s Advice of Local Check Disbursements of the city government. See to it that copies of Accountant’s Advice in the ensuing period are regularly and completely submitted to the Audit Team as mandated in COA Circular No. 96-007 dated May 15, 1996. We suggested that it must be forwarded to the Auditor on a weekly basis for all funds per bank account check issuances.

We recommended that Management require the ICO-City Treasurer and Liquidating Officer to ensure that all collections and issued accountable forms are closely reviewed, scrutinized and where necessary, recalculated in order to avoid incurrence of under collections/under remittances. Require the Collecting Clerks to remit the amount of audit charge. Strict adherence to the above-mentioned guidelines on collections and deposits is required.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to Sections 7, 8 and 9 of COA Circular No. 97-001 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 governing the rendition of accounts and submission of copies of contracts is required. Direct the BAC Secretariat, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to closely coordinate in the compliance of the required documentation and submission to the Audit Team. Further, it is suggested that the Office of the City Accountant require the attachment of complete supporting documents on the duly processed disbursement vouchers pertaining to infrastructure projects before effecting the payment.

a) For the issue on Annual membership dues: We recommended that the Management require the ICO, City Treasurer to demand for the issuance of the Official Receipt from the League of the Cities of the Philippines covering the payment of £150,000.00 as support to the disbursement made. Henceforth, we suggested that the LGU must not deposit directly to the League Bank account and not to issue the check unless there is an official receipt issued to acknowledge the payment in subsequent transactions.

b) For fund transfers/financial assistance: We recommended that Management ensure that all financial assistance are covered by a Memorandum of Agreement setting forth the scope of project/programs/activities and requirement for the submission of fund utilization reports/liquidation reports pursuant to COA Circular No. 2007-001.

c) For financial assistance not properly taken up: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to record receivable account from the League to ensure fair presentation of the account.

Henceforth, for control purposes, ensure that the transfer is properly taken up in the books of both agencies, the transferred funds are used only for the intended purpose, and that proper accounting and reporting is made of the utilization of the funds.

We recommended that the Office of the City Treasurer conduct intensification of its revenue collection campaign and install appropriate monitoring mechanisms to ensure efficient and effective collection of targeted revenues.

We suggested that the Offices of the City Assessor and City Treasurer coordinate and install measures on how to strengthen the monitoring mechanisms relative to taxes due on real properties and the delinquent taxpayers. Moreover, strengthen the internal control system in the reporting and recording process of collections to eliminate errors and increase actual collections of revenues due for the city government.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management: (a) Cause for the immediate deposit of collections amounting to $1,923,859.54, otherwise the same will be considered as shortage of the accountable officers concerned.
observations.managementAction: Collections totaling $3,665,441.66 remained undeposited as of December 31, 2016. The Honorable Mayor called the attention of all concerned Accountable Officers to settle immediately their accounts. Partial remittances of prior year’s balance were made during the year.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the management of LGU-Masbate City: (a) Strictly enforce settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2015, particularly those of the previous years. (b) Stop the granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled/liquidated to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances. Send demand letters to all concerned requiring the immediate settlement and/or refund of the amount advanced, and enforce the withholding of salaries of those who fail to settle/liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period after the demand. (d) Initiate appropriate legal/disciplinary action against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws and regulations, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant continued to grant Cash Advances to employees with unsettledand outstanding Cash Advances. (Please see related finding per Audit Observation No. 2). Demand letters were issued to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances. Liquidation of some employees were settled, thus reducing the balance of outstanding Cash Advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the City Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements within ten days from receipt of the Monthly Bank Statements for the Auditor to have ample time in the verification of the report. See to it that the Cash in Bank balances in the books are already adjusted and reconciled with the records/cashbooks of the City Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: No adjustment has been made for the reconciling items per Bank Reconciliation Statements for the General Fund only. But, the Office of the City Accountant had furnished the Audit Team with the missing disbursement vouchers omitted in the RCIs but debited per bank statements. However, the accompanying statements were not submitted together with the report. In this regard, the LCE required the Accounting Office to take action on this finding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency strictly comply with the proper procedure and accounting treatment in the recording of Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables as prescribed under Sections 246 & 248 of R.A. 7160 and Section 20 of the New Government Accounting System manual for Local Government Units, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: Evaluation of CY 2016 General Ledger showed that the agency had set up on Jan. 1, 2016 the realty receivable accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant record in the books of accounts the cost of the equipment and make the necessary adjusting entries in order to present the true financial position of the City Government. The Management and the concerned City Officials are also reminded of the administrative or criminal liability which may be imposed as a consequence of its failure to account without justifiable cause.
observations.managementAction: Evaluation of records showed that the aforementioned properties were accordingly recorded in the books which resulted to an increase of the Construction & Heavy Equipment account as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Management direct the City General Service Officer (CGSO), City Accountant and Inventory Committee to finish the physical inventory of the City’s properties and to furnish the Auditor with a copy of the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to facilitate the verification and audit of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts. Properties found to be unserviceable should be disposed of immediately.
observations.managementAction: Partial report was submitted by the CGSO but considered as a mere listing only of the assets owned by the city. The LCE required the GSO and other concerned officials to conduct the inventory of all properties of the city based on the office records. The City General Services Officer (CGSO) and Inventory Committee were not able to complete the conduct of the required inventory. (Please see related finding per Audit Observation No. 3).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management remit in full and within the prescribed period all taxes withheld from individuals and suppliers in accordance with Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 and Republic Act No. 8424.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2016, the account Due to BIR still has a balance of $9,870,795.71. Prior year’s accounts were already remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chief Executive require the City Engineer and the City Planning Development Coordinator to provide the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year. Also, require them to prepare and submit to the Audit Team every end of the quarter the report on publicized PPAs for monitoring as well as auditing purposes of the status of implementation of each project.
observations.managementAction: Not all on-going projects are reflected in the report. Separate report should have been prepared at the onset of the calendar year. Some of the on-going PPAs were instead reported in the Quarterly Report of Publicized PPAs.
